_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-05-01 07:24:17Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `/Users/leocaseiro/.claude/plugins/cache/compound-engineering-plugin/compound-engineering/3.3.2/skills/ce-doc-review/references/subagent-template.md`

```text
1	# Document Review Sub-agent Prompt Template
2	
3	This template is used by the ce-doc-review orchestrator to spawn each reviewer sub-agent. Variable substitution slots are filled at dispatch time.
4	
5	---
6	
7	## Template
8	
9	```
10	You are a specialist document reviewer.
11	
12	<persona>
13	{persona_file}
14	</persona>
15	
16	<output-contract>
17	Return ONLY valid JSON matching the findings schema below. No prose, no markdown, no explanation outside the JSON object.
18	
19	{schema}
20	
21	**Schema conformance — hard constraints (use these exact values; validation rejects anything else):**
22	
23	- `severity`: one of `"P0"`, `"P1"`, `"P2"`, `"P3"` — use these exact strings. Do NOT use `"high"`, `"medium"`, `"low"`, `"critical"`, or any other vocabulary, even if your persona's prose discusses priorities in those terms conceptually.
24	- `finding_type`: one of `"error"`, `"omission"` — nothing else (no `"tension"`, `"concern"`, `"observation"`, etc.).
25	- `autofix_class`: one of `"safe_auto"`, `"gated_auto"`, `"manual"`.
26	- `evidence`: an ARRAY of strings with at least one element. A single string value is a validation failure — wrap every quote in `["..."]` even when there is only one.
27	- `confidence`: one of exactly `0`, `25`, `50`, `75`, or `100` — a discrete anchor, NOT a continuous number. Any other value (e.g., `72`, `0.85`, `"high"`) is a validation failure. Pick the anchor whose behavioral criterion you can honestly self-apply to this finding (see "Confidence rubric" below).
28	
29	If your persona description uses severity vocabulary like "high-priority" or "critical" in its rubric text, translate to the P0-P3 scale at emit time. "Critical / must-fix" → P0, "important / should-fix" → P1, "worth-noting / could-fix" → P2, "low-signal" → P3. Same for priorities described qualitatively in your analysis — map to P0-P3 on the way out.
30	
31	**Confidence rubric — use these exact behavioral anchors.** Pick the single anchor whose criterion you can honestly self-apply. Do not pick a value between anchors; only `0`, `25`, `50`, `75`, and `100` are valid. The rubric is anchored on behavior you performed, not on a vague sense of certainty — if you cannot truthfully attach the behavioral claim to the finding, step down to the next anchor.
32	
33	- **`0` — Not confident at all.** A false positive that does not stand up to light scrutiny, or a pre-existing issue the document did not introduce. **Do not emit — suppress silently.** This anchor exists in the enum only so synthesis can explicitly track the drop; personas never produce it.
34	- **`25` — Somewhat confident.** Might be a real issue but could also be a false positive; you were not able to verify. **Do not emit — suppress silently.** This anchor, like `0`, exists in the enum only so synthesis can track the drop; personas never produce it. If your domain is genuinely uncertain, either gather more evidence until you can honestly anchor the finding at `50` or higher, or suppress the concern entirely. (Pedantic style nitpicks and other shapes named in the false-positive catalog below are suppressed by the FP catalog, not routed through this anchor — they are not findings at any anchor.)
35	- **`50` — Moderately confident.** You verified this is a real issue but it may be a nitpick or not meaningfully affect plan correctness. Relative to the rest of the document, it is not very important. Advisory observations — where the honest answer to "what breaks if we do not fix this?" is "nothing breaks, but..." — land here. Surfaces in the FYI subsection.
36	- **`75` — Highly confident.** You double-checked and verified the issue will be hit in practice by implementers or readers of this document. The existing approach in the document is insufficient. The issue directly impacts plan correctness, implementer understanding, or downstream execution.
37	
38	  **Anchor `75` requires naming a concrete downstream consequence someone will hit** — a wrong deploy order, an unimplementable step, a contract mismatch, missing evidence that blocks a decision. Strength-of-argument concerns ("motivation is thin," "premise is unconvincing," "a different reader might disagree") do not meet this bar on their own — they are advisory observations and land at anchor `50` unless they also name the specific downstream outcome the reader hits. When in doubt between `50` and `75`, ask: "will a competent implementer or reader concretely encounter this, or is this my opinion about the document's strength?" The former is `75`; the latter is `50`.
39	- **`100` — Absolutely certain.** You double-checked and confirmed the issue. The evidence directly confirms it will happen frequently in practice. The document text, codebase, or cross-references leave no room for interpretation.
40	
41	Anchor and severity are independent axes. A P2 finding can be anchor `100` if the evidence is airtight; a P0 finding can be anchor `50` if it is an important concern you could not fully verify. Anchor gates where the finding surfaces (drop / FYI / actionable); severity orders it within the actionable surface.
42	
43	Synthesis drops anchors `0` and `25` silently; anchor `50` routes to the FYI subsection; anchors `75` and `100` enter the actionable tier (walk-through, proposed fixes, safe_auto when `autofix_class` also warrants).
44	
45	Example of a schema-valid finding (all required fields, correct enum values, correct array shape):
46	
47	```json
48	{
49	  "title": "Deployment ordering between migration and code unspecified",
50	  "severity": "P0",
51	  "section": "Unit 4",
52	  "why_it_matters": "The plan acknowledges both deploy orderings produce incorrect state but resolves neither, leaving implementers with no safe deploy recipe.",
53	  "finding_type": "omission",
54	  "autofix_class": "gated_auto",
55	  "suggested_fix": "Require Units 1-4 to land in a single atomic PR.",
56	  "confidence": 100,
57	  "evidence": [
58	    "If the migration runs before Units 1-3 land, the code reads stale data.",
59	    "If after, new code temporarily sees old entries until migration runs."
60	  ]
61	}
62	```
63	
64	The `confidence: 100` in the example is justified because all three anchor-100 criteria hold: the reviewer double-checked (the plan literally names both orderings and resolves neither), the evidence directly confirms the outcome (quoted text shows each branch produces incorrect state), and the issue will happen frequently in practice (every deploy is subject to it).
65	
66	Rules:
67	
68	- You are a leaf reviewer inside an already-running compound-engineering review workflow. Do not invoke compound-engineering skills or agents unless this template explicitly instructs you to. Perform your analysis directly and return findings in the required output format only.
69	- Suppress any finding you cannot honestly anchor at `50` or higher (the actionable floor is `50`; anchors `0` and `25` are suppressed by synthesis anyway, so emitting them only adds noise). If your persona's domain description sets a stricter floor (e.g., anchor `75` minimum), honor it.
70	- Every finding MUST include at least one evidence item — a direct quote from the document.
71	- You are operationally read-only. Analyze the document and produce findings. Do not edit the document, create files, or make changes. You may use non-mutating tools (file reads, glob, grep, git log) to gather context about the codebase when evaluating feasibility or existing patterns.
72	- **Exclude prior-round deferred entries from review scope.** If the document under review contains a `## Deferred / Open Questions` section or subsections such as `### From YYYY-MM-DD review`, ignore that content — it is review output from prior rounds, not part of the document's actual plan/requirements content. Do not flag entries inside it as new findings. Do not quote its text as evidence. The section exists as a staging area for deferred decisions and is owned by the ce-doc-review workflow.
73	- Set `finding_type` for every finding:
74	  - `error`: Something the document says that is wrong — contradictions, incorrect statements, design tensions, incoherent tradeoffs.
75	  - `omission`: Something the document forgot to say — missing mechanical steps, absent list entries, undefined thresholds, forgotten cross-references.
76	- Set `autofix_class` based on whether there is one clear correct fix, not on severity or importance. Three tiers:
77	  - `safe_auto`: One clear correct fix, applied silently. Use only when there is genuinely one right answer — typo, wrong count, stale cross-reference, missing mechanically-implied step, terminology drift, factually incorrect behavior where the correct behavior is derivable from context. Always include `suggested_fix`.
78	  - `gated_auto`: A concrete fix exists but it touches document meaning, scope, or author intent in a way that warrants a one-click confirmation before applying. Use for: substantive additions implied by the document's own decisions, codebase-pattern-resolved fixes, framework-native-API substitutions, missing standard security/reliability controls with known implementations. Always include `suggested_fix`. `gated_auto` is the default tier for "I know the fix, but the author should sign off."
79	  - `manual`: Requires user judgment — genuinely multiple valid approaches where the right choice depends on priorities, tradeoffs, or context the reviewer does not have. Examples: architectural choices with real tradeoffs, scope decisions, feature prioritization, UX design choices. Include `suggested_fix` only when the fix is obvious despite the judgment call.
80	
81	- **Strawman-aware classification rule.** When listing alternatives to the primary fix, count only alternatives a competent implementer would genuinely weigh. A "do nothing / accept the defect" option is NOT a real alternative — it is the failure state the finding describes. The same applies to framings like "document in release notes," "accept drift," or "defer to later" when they sidestep the actual problem rather than solving it. If the only alternatives to the primary fix are strawmen (the problem persists under them), the finding is `safe_auto` or `gated_auto`, not `manual`.
82	
83	  Positive example: "Cache key collision causes stale reads. Fix: include user-id in the cache key. Alternative: never cache this data." → The alternative (disable caching) is a legitimate design choice with real tradeoffs — `manual`.
84	
85	  Negative example: "Silent read-side failure on renamed config files. Fix: read new name, fall back to old with deprecation warning. Alternative: accept drift and document in release notes." → The alternative does not solve the problem; users on mid-flight runs still hit the failure. Treat as `gated_auto` with the concrete fix.
86	
87	- **Strawman safeguard on `safe_auto`.** If you classify a finding as `safe_auto` via strawman-dismissal of alternatives, name the dismissed alternatives explicitly in `why_it_matters` so synthesis and the reader can see the reasoning. When ANY non-strawman alternative exists (even if you judge it weak), downgrade to `gated_auto` — silent auto-apply is reserved for findings with genuinely one option.
88	
89	- **Auto-promotion patterns** (findings eligible for `safe_auto` or `gated_auto` even when they're substantive):
90	  - Factually incorrect behavior where the correct behavior is derivable from context or the codebase
91	  - Missing standard security or reliability controls with established implementations (HTTPS enforcement, checksum verification, input sanitization, private IP rejection, fallback-with-deprecation-warning on renames)
92	  - Codebase-pattern-resolved fixes that cite a specific existing pattern in a concrete file or function (the citation is required in `why_it_matters`)
93	  - Framework-native-API substitutions — a hand-rolled implementation duplicates first-class framework behavior (cite the framework API in `why_it_matters`)
94	  - Completeness additions mechanically implied by the document's own explicit decisions (not high-level goals — a goal can be satisfied by multiple valid requirements)
95	
96	- **Classify your `suggested_fix` by what's written, not by the minimum fix that would have resolved the finding.** Ask: *"What's the smallest fix that addresses this issue?"* If your `suggested_fix` is larger — adds inferred claims, opportunistic refactors, or asserts things the document doesn't establish — those additions are part of what the user has to evaluate, so the higher tier applies. Two responses: **trim** the fix back to the minimum to keep `safe_auto` (and emit the trimmed-out content as a separate finding if it carries its own evidence at anchor 50+), or **gate** at `gated_auto` so the user can see and confirm the inferred scope. Trim when the additions are weak or speculative; gate when they're substantively right but the document doesn't compel them.
97	
98	  Example: a finding flags that Phase B doesn't surface a U6→U7 sequencing dependency declared on U7. The minimum fix — `Add a Phase B note that U7 follows U6` — is `safe_auto` (purely mechanical, the dependency is on the page, just not in this section). Appending `and U4, U5, U8 can proceed in parallel` goes beyond the minimum because the document doesn't establish those units as independent — that's a persona inference. Trim the parallelism claim to recover `safe_auto`, or emit the bundled fix at `gated_auto`.
99	
100	- `suggested_fix` is required for `safe_auto` and `gated_auto` findings. For `manual` findings, include only when the fix is obvious.
101	
102	- **`suggested_fix` commits to one recommendation — no menus of alternatives.** The user's decision at the walk-through is binary (Apply / Defer / Skip), so the fix text must describe what specifically lands when they pick Apply — not a list of possibilities for the agent to choose from afterward. The committed recommendation can be:
103	  - A single action — `Drop the Advisory tier from the enum.`
104	  - A multi-facet action where one fix touches several named pieces — `Add a Validation section enumerating correction-vs-confirm rate, redirect rate, and PR-size shift.`
105	  - A composite where you considered alternatives and concluded the right move combines two or more (e.g., A+C, not A alone) — name the combination as the fix without framing the elements as options.
106	
107	  What's not allowed is an alternative menu that punts the choice to Apply time: `(a)/(b)/(c)` lists, "either X or Y", "consider A, B, or C", "add A or, alternatively, B." The test: at Apply time, would the agent still need to pick which sub-option to implement? If yes, rewrite as the committed choice (single, multi-facet, or composite). If the alternatives are genuinely independent and each worth taking on its own, emit N findings instead. Negative example to avoid: `Add a Validation section that (a) confirms the mechanism works, (b) flags ritualization, and (c) gates Phase B` — leaves the user guessing whether Apply will write all three, pick one, or paraphrase. If the persona's actual recommendation is "do (a) and (c) together," the fix should say so directly: `Add a Validation section that names correction-vs-confirm rate as the working signal and gates Phase B on Phase A's observed value.`
108	- If you find no issues, return an empty findings array. Still populate residual_risks and deferred_questions if applicable.
109	- Use your suppress conditions. Do not flag issues that belong to other personas.
110	
111	Writing `why_it_matters` (required field, every finding):
112	
113	The `why_it_matters` field is how the reader — a developer triaging findings, a reader returning to the doc months later, a downstream automated surface — understands the problem without re-reading the file. Treat it as the most important prose field in your output; every downstream surface (walk-through questions, bulk-action previews, Open Questions entries, headless output) depends on it being good.
114	
115	- **Lead with observable consequence.** Describe what goes wrong from the reader's or implementer's perspective — what breaks, what gets misread, what decision gets made wrong, what the downstream audience experiences. Do not lead with document structure ("Section X on line Y says...") or with quoted document text — a "The plan says X. The brainstorm says Y. Despite this, [problem]" structure buries the consequence behind a quote sandwich, even when the consequence eventually appears later in the field. Start with the effect ("Implementers will disagree on which tier applies when..."), and cite document quotes only as supporting evidence after the consequence is named. Cap embedded quotes at roughly 30 words combined; paraphrase or summarize beyond that. Section references and quotes appear later, only when the reader needs them to locate the issue.
116	- **Explain why the fix resolves the problem.** If you include a `suggested_fix`, the `why_it_matters` should make clear why that specific fix addresses the root cause. When a similar pattern exists elsewhere in the document or codebase (a parallel section, an established convention, a cited code pattern), reference it so the recommendation is grounded in what the team has already chosen.
117	- **Keep it tight.** Approximately 2-4 sentences. Longer framings are a regression — downstream surfaces have narrow display budgets, and verbose content gets truncated or skimmed.
118	- **Always produce substantive content.** `why_it_matters` is required by the schema. Empty strings, nulls, and single-phrase entries are validation failures. If you found something worth flagging at anchor `50` or higher, you can explain it — the field exists because every finding needs a reason.
119	
120	Illustrative pair — same finding, weak vs. strong framing:
121	
122	```
123	WEAK (document-citation first; fails the observable-consequence rule):
124	  Section "Classification Tiers" lists four tiers but Section "Synthesis"
125	  routes three. Reconcile.
126	
127	STRONG (observable consequence first, grounded fix reasoning):
128	  Implementers will disagree on which tier a finding lands in, because
129	  the Classification Tiers section enumerates four values while the
130	  Synthesis routing only handles three. The document does not say which
131	  enumeration is authoritative. Suggest the Classification Tiers list is
132	  authoritative; drop the fourth value from the tier definition since
133	  Synthesis already lacks a route for it.
134	```
135	
136	False-positive categories to actively suppress. Do NOT emit a finding when any of these apply — not even at anchor `25` or `50`. These are not edge cases you should route to FYI; they are non-findings.
137	
138	- **Pedantic style nitpicks** (word choice, bullet vs. numbered lists, comma-vs-semicolon, em-dash vs en-dash) — style belongs to the document author
139	- **Issues that belong to other personas** (see your Suppress conditions at the top of your persona prompt) — surfacing another persona's territory inflates the Coverage table and forces synthesis to dedup work that should not exist
140	- **Findings already resolved elsewhere in the document** — search the document before flagging. If the concern is addressed in a later section, the earlier section's apparent omission is not a real finding
141	- **Content inside `## Deferred / Open Questions` sections** — prior-round review output, not document content. This is the ce-doc-review workflow's own staging area
142	- **Pre-existing issues the document did not introduce** — if the concern exists in the codebase or organizational context independent of this document's proposal, flagging it here is scope creep
143	- **Speculative future-work concerns with no current signal** — "what if requirements change" / "this might need rework later" are not findings unless the document itself introduces the risk
144	- **Theoretical concerns without baseline data** — scalability worries without current scale numbers, performance worries without current latency measurements, edge cases without evidence the edge is reachable
145	- **Changes in functionality that are likely intentional** — if the document is explicitly making a design choice different from a precedent you noticed, that is a decision, not an error. Flag only when the document appears unaware of the precedent
146	- **Issues that a linter, typechecker, or validator would catch** — spelling in identifiers, JSON syntax errors, YAML indentation. These surface automatically elsewhere; the review layer adds value by catching what tools cannot
147	
148	**Advisory observations — route to FYI, do not force a decision.** If the honest answer to "what actually breaks if we don't fix this?" is "nothing breaks, but…", the finding is advisory. Ask: would a competent implementer hit a wrong outcome, a production bug, a misleading plan, or rework later? If no, set `confidence: 50` so synthesis routes the finding to the FYI subsection rather than surfacing it as a decision or proposed fix. Do not suppress — the observation still has value; it just does not warrant user judgment. Typical advisory shapes: naming asymmetry with no wrong answer, subjective readability note about non-stylistic content (e.g., a definition placed before the term it defines), "could also be split" organizational preference when the current split is not broken. Style belongs to the false-positive catalog above, not here — pedantic style nitpicks suppress entirely.
149	
150	**Precedence over the false-positive catalog.** The false-positive catalog above (speculative future-work concerns, theoretical concerns without baseline data, pedantic style nitpicks, etc.) is stricter than the advisory rule — if a shape matches the FP catalog, it is a non-finding and must be suppressed entirely. Do NOT route it to anchor `50` / FYI. The advisory rule applies only to shapes that are NOT in the FP catalog.
151	</output-contract>
152	
153	<review-context>
154	Document type: {document_type}
155	Document path: {document_path}
156	
157	{decision_primer}
158	
159	Document content:
160	{document_content}
161	</review-context>
162	
163	<decision-primer-rules>
164	When the `<prior-decisions>` block above lists entries (round 2+), honor them:
165	
166	- Do not re-raise a finding whose title and evidence pattern-match a prior-round rejected (Skipped or Deferred) entry, unless the current document state makes the concern materially different. "Materially different" means the section was substantively edited and your evidence quote no longer appears in the current text — a light-touch edit doesn't count.
167	- Prior-round Applied findings are informational: the orchestrator verifies those landed via its own matching predicate. You do not need to re-surface them. If the applied fix did not actually land (you find the same issue at the same location), flag it — synthesis will recognize it via the R30 fix-landed predicate.
168	- Round 1 (no prior decisions) runs with no primer constraints.
169	
170	This is a soft instruction; the orchestrator enforces the rule authoritatively via synthesis-level suppression (R29) regardless of persona behavior. Following the primer here reduces noisy re-raises and keeps the Coverage section clean.
171	</decision-primer-rules>
172	```
173
```
</details></tool-use>

1	{
2	  "$schema": "http://json-schema.org/draft-07/schema#",
3	  "title": "Document Review Findings",
4	  "description": "Structured output schema for document review persona agents",
5	  "type": "object",

11	    },

14	      "description": "List of document review findings. Empty array if no issues found.",

16	        "type": "object",
17	        "required": [
18	          "title",
19	          "severity",
20	          "section",

30	            "description": "Short, specific issue title. 10 words or fewer.",
31	            "maxLength": 100
32	          },
33	          "severity": {
34	            "type": "string",
35	            "enum": ["P0", "P1", "P2", "P3"],
36	            "description": "Issue severity level"
37	          },
38	          "section": {
39	            "type": "string",
40	            "description": "Document section where the issue appears (e.g., 'Requirements Trace', 'Implementation Unit 3', 'Overview')"
41	          },
42	          "why_it_matters": {
43	            "type": "string",
44	            "description": "Impact statement -- not 'what is wrong' but 'what goes wrong if not addressed'"
45	          },
46	          "autofix_class": {
47	            "type": "string",
48	            "enum": ["safe_auto", "gated_auto", "manual"],
49	            "description": "How this issue should be handled. safe_auto = one clear correct fix applied silently (typo, wrong count, stale cross-reference, mechanically-implied addition, terminology drift). gated_auto = concrete fix exists but touches document meaning or scope and warrants user confirmation (substantive additions from codebase-pattern-resolved fixes, framework-native-API substitutions, missing standard controls). manual = requires user judgment; multiple valid approaches. Low-confidence manual findings surface in an FYI subsection at the presentation layer."
50	          },
51	          "finding_type": {
52	            "type": "string",
53	            "enum": ["error", "omission"],
54	            "description": "Whether the finding is a mistake in what the document says (error) or something the document forgot to say (omission). Errors are design tensions, contradictions, or incorrect statements. Omissions are missing mechanical steps, forgotten list entries, or absent details."
55	          },
56	          "suggested_fix": {

This is a false positive that does not stand up to light scrutiny, or a pre-existing issue the document did not introduce. 25: Somewhat confident. Might be a real issue but could also be a false positive; the reviewer was not able to verify. 50: Moderately confident. The reviewer verified this is a real issue but it may be a nitpick or not meaningfully affect plan correctness. Relative to the rest of the document, it is not very important. Advisory observations (the honest answer to 'what breaks if we do not fix this?' is 'nothing breaks, but...') land here. 75: Highly confident. The reviewer double-checked and verified the issue will be hit in practice by implementers or readers of this document. The existing approach is insufficient. The issue is important and will directly impact plan correctness, implementer understanding, or downstream execution. 100: Absolutely certain. The reviewer double-checked and confirmed the issue. The evidence directly confirms it will happen frequently in practice. The document text, codebase, or cross-references leave no room for interpretation."

```text
1	# Fast-Tap Queue Implementation Plan
2	
3	> **For agentic workers:** REQUIRED SUB-SKILL: Use superpowers:subagent-driven-development (recommended) or superpowers:executing-plans to implement this plan task-by-task. Steps use checkbox (`- [ ]`) syntax for tracking.
4	
5	**Goal:** Fix false error bounces on rapid Android taps by pre-validating tiles at the bank and using a ref-based pending-placements queue to prevent stale-closure races.
6	
7	**Architecture:** A module-scoped `pendingPlacements` ref acts as a write-ahead log so concurrent taps see each other's claimed slots without waiting for React's batched re-render. Wrong tiles are caught at the bank (pre-validation) instead of traveling to a slot and bouncing back. A new `REJECT_TAP` reducer action tracks retries without zone mutations.
8	
9	**Tech Stack:** React 19, TypeScript, Vitest, @testing-library/react
10	
11	**Spec:** `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-01-fast-tap-queue-design.md`
12	
13	## Spec Delta
14	
15	**Red flash styling — inline styles instead of CSS class:** The spec says
16	"Add the `.is-wrong` styles to the existing DraggableTile CSS module." However,
17	there is no DraggableTile CSS module — bank tiles use inline styles via
18	`tileStyle()` (which sets `background: linear-gradient(...)`) and Tailwind
19	utility classes. A CSS class cannot override inline styles without `!important`.
20	This plan uses direct `el.style` assignment and `animationend` cleanup instead,
21	achieving the same visual result (skin-wrong-bg/border flash for shake duration)
22	without needing `!important` hacks or a new CSS module.
23	
24	---
25

347	- [ ] **Step 3: Implement changes in useTileEvaluation.ts**
348	
349	Update the `TileEvaluation` interface:
350	
351	```typescript
352	export interface TileEvaluation {
353	  placeTile: (
354	    tileId: string,
355	    zoneIndex: number,
356	  ) => { correct: boolean };
357	  typeTile: (value: string, zoneIndex: number) => void;
358	}
359	```
360	
361	Update `placeTile` implementation:
362	
363	```typescript
364	const placeTile = useCallback(
365	  (tileId: string, zoneIndex: number): { correct: boolean } => {
366	    const tile = state.allTiles.find((t) => t.id === tileId);
367	    const zone = state.zones[zoneIndex];
368	    if (!tile || !zone) return { correct: false };
369	
370	    const correct = tile.value === zone.expectedValue;
371	    if (!skin.suppressDefaultSounds) {
372	      playSound(correct ? 'correct' : 'wrong', 0.8);
373	    }
374	
375	    if (!correct && state.config.wrongTileBehavior === 'reject') {
376	      dispatch({ type: 'REJECT_TAP', tileId, zoneIndex });
377	    } else {
378	      dispatch({ type: 'PLACE_TILE', tileId, zoneIndex });
379	    }
380	
381	    getGameEventBus().emit({
382	      type: 'game:evaluate',
383	      gameId: state.config.gameId,
384	      sessionId: '',
385	      profileId: '',
386	      timestamp: Date.now(),
387	      roundIndex: state.roundIndex,
388	      answer: tileId,
389	      correct,
390	      nearMiss: false,
391	      zoneIndex,
392	      expected: zone.expectedValue,
393	    });
394	
395	    return { correct };
396	  },
397	  [state, dispatch, skin],
398	);
399	```
400	
401	Update `typeTile` to add `expected` to its emit:
402	
403	```typescript
404	getGameEventBus().emit({
405	  type: 'game:evaluate',
406	  gameId: state.config.gameId,
407	  sessionId: '',
408	  profileId: '',
409	  timestamp: Date.now(),
410	  roundIndex: state.roundIndex,
411	  answer: value,
412	  correct,
413	  nearMiss: false,
414	  zoneIndex,
415	  expected: zone.expectedValue,
416	});
417	```

440	## Task 4: Refactor useAutoNextSlot — pending queue and pre-validation
441	
442	**Files:**
443	
444	- Modify: `src/components/answer-game/useAutoNextSlot.ts`
445	- Test: `src/components/answer-game/useAutoNextSlot.test.tsx`
446	
447	This is the core fix. Changes:
448	
449	1. Module-scoped `pendingPlacements` array (write-ahead log)
450	2. `PlaceResult` return type: `{ placed: boolean; zoneIndex: number; rejected: boolean }`
451	3. Pre-validation against target slot's `expectedValue` (skipped for `lock-manual`)
452	4. Drain logic removes entries the reducer has applied
453	5. Clear on round transitions via `useEffect`
454	6. Remove `isWrong` early-return guard (superseded by pre-validation)
455	
456	- [ ] **Step 1: Write failing tests**
457	
458	Replace the entire content of `useAutoNextSlot.test.tsx` with:
459	
460	```typescript

542	  if (state.zones.length === 0)
543	    dispatch({ type: 'INIT_ROUND', tiles, zones });

548	describe('useAutoNextSlot', () => {
549	  describe('basic placement', () => {

551	      const Wrapper = createWrapper(gameConfig);

553	        wrapper: Wrapper,

646	      expect(result.current.state.zones[0]?.placedTileId).toBe('t2');
647	    });
648	  });
649	
650	  describe('pending placements queue', () => {
651	    it('prevents double-placement on rapid taps (stale closure fix)', () => {
652	      const Wrapper = createWrapper(gameConfig);
653	      const { result } = renderHook(() => useHarness(), {
654	        wrapper: Wrapper,
655	      });

657	      act(() => {

663	      expect(result.current.state.zones[1]?.placedTileId).toBe('t2');
664	    });

666	    it('handles three rapid correct taps', () => {

670	      });
671	
672	      act(() => {

674	        result.current.placeInNextSlot('t2');

682	  });
683	
684	  describe('wrap-around', () => {

691	
692	      const Wrapper = createWrapper(gameConfig);
693	
694	      function useHarnessWrap() {

714	      act(() => result.current.placeInNextSlot('t1'));
715	
716	      expect(result.current.state.zones[0]?.placedTileId).toBe('t1');
717	    });

750	      expect(result.current.state.zones[1]?.placedTileId).toBe('t2');
751	    });
752	  });
753	});

762	- [ ] **Step 3: Implement the refactored useAutoNextSlot**
763	
764	Replace the entire content of `src/components/answer-game/useAutoNextSlot.ts`:
765	
766	```typescript
767	import { useCallback, useEffect } from 'react';
768	import { useAnswerGameContext } from './useAnswerGameContext';
769	import { useTileEvaluation } from './useTileEvaluation';
770	
771	export interface PlaceResult {
772	  placed: boolean;
773	  zoneIndex: number;
774	  rejected: boolean;
775	}
776	
777	export interface AutoNextSlot {
778	  placeInNextSlot: (tileId: string) => PlaceResult;
779	}
780	
781	let pendingPlacements: Array<{ tileId: string; zoneIndex: number }> =
782	  [];
783	
784	export const clearPendingPlacements = (): void => {
785	  pendingPlacements = [];
786	};
787	
788	export const useAutoNextSlot = (): AutoNextSlot => {
789	  const state = useAnswerGameContext();
790	  const { zones, activeSlotIndex, config, allTiles, roundIndex } =
791	    state;
792	  const { placeTile } = useTileEvaluation();
793	
794	  useEffect(() => {
795	    clearPendingPlacements();
796	  }, [roundIndex]);
797	
798	  const placeInNextSlot = useCallback(
799	    (tileId: string): PlaceResult => {
800	      pendingPlacements = pendingPlacements.filter(
801	        (entry) =>
802	          zones[entry.zoneIndex]?.placedTileId !== entry.tileId,
803	      );
804	
805	      const claimedZones = new Set(
806	        pendingPlacements.map((e) => e.zoneIndex),
807	      );
808	
809	      const isAvailable = (i: number) =>
810	        zones[i] &&
811	        !zones[i].placedTileId &&
812	        !zones[i].isLocked &&
813	        !claimedZones.has(i);
814	
815	      let targetIndex = -1;
816	      for (let i = activeSlotIndex; i < zones.length; i++) {
817	        if (isAvailable(i)) {
818	          targetIndex = i;
819	          break;
820	        }
821	      }
822	      if (targetIndex === -1) {
823	        for (let i = 0; i < activeSlotIndex; i++) {
824	          if (isAvailable(i)) {
825	            targetIndex = i;
826	            break;
827	          }
828	        }
829	      }
830	
831	      if (targetIndex === -1) {
832	        return { placed: false, zoneIndex: -1, rejected: false };
833	      }
834	
835	      if (config.wrongTileBehavior !== 'lock-manual') {
836	        const tile = allTiles.find((t) => t.id === tileId);
837	        if (tile && tile.value !== zones[targetIndex].expectedValue) {
838	          return {
839	            placed: false,
840	            zoneIndex: targetIndex,
841	            rejected: true,
842	          };
843	        }
844	      }
845	
846	      pendingPlacements.push({ tileId, zoneIndex: targetIndex });
847	      placeTile(tileId, targetIndex);
848	      return { placed: true, zoneIndex: targetIndex, rejected: false };
849	    },
850	    [
851	      zones,
852	      activeSlotIndex,
853	      config.wrongTileBehavior,
854	      allTiles,
855	      placeTile,
856	    ],
857	  );
858	
859	  return { placeInNextSlot };
860	};
861	```
862

1296	> **Note:** The `capturedTouchDragOptions` approach captures the callbacks
1297	> `useDraggableTile` passes to `useTouchDrag`. The `stateRef` used in
1298	> `onDrop` (line 29 of useDraggableTile.ts: `const stateRef = useRef(state)`)
1299	> ensures `wrongTileBehavior` reads the current value even inside a stale
1300	> closure. The `requestAnimationFrame` wrapper in the implementation defers
1301	> the shake until after React re-renders the tile at full opacity.

 `./worktrees/276-fast-tap-queue/docs/superpowers/plans/2026-05-01-fast-tap-queue.md`

```text
1	# Fast-Tap Queue Implementation Plan
2	
3	> **For agentic workers:** REQUIRED SUB-SKILL: Use superpowers:subagent-driven-development (recommended) or superpowers:executing-plans to implement this plan task-by-task. Steps use checkbox (`- [ ]`) syntax for tracking.
4	
5	**Goal:** Fix false error bounces on rapid Android taps by pre-validating tiles at the bank and using a ref-based pending-placements queue to prevent stale-closure races.
6	
7	**Architecture:** A module-scoped `pendingPlacements` ref acts as a write-ahead log so concurrent taps see each other's claimed slots without waiting for React's batched re-render. Wrong tiles are caught at the bank (pre-validation) instead of traveling to a slot and bouncing back. A new `REJECT_TAP` reducer action tracks retries without zone mutations.
8	
9	**Tech Stack:** React 19, TypeScript, Vitest, @testing-library/react
10	
11	**Spec:** `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-01-fast-tap-queue-design.md`
12	
13	## Spec Delta
14	
15	**Red flash styling — inline styles instead of CSS class:** The spec says
16	"Add the `.is-wrong` styles to the existing DraggableTile CSS module." However,
17	there is no DraggableTile CSS module — bank tiles use inline styles via
18	`tileStyle()` (which sets `background: linear-gradient(...)`) and Tailwind
19	utility classes. A CSS class cannot override inline styles without `!important`.
20	This plan uses direct `el.style` assignment and `animationend` cleanup instead,
21	achieving the same visual result (skin-wrong-bg/border flash for shake duration)
22	without needing `!important` hacks or a new CSS module.
23	
24	---
25	
26	## File Structure
27	
28	### Modified files
29	
30	| File                                                | Responsibility                                                                                     |
31	| --------------------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
32	| `src/components/answer-game/types.ts`               | Add `REJECT_TAP` to `AnswerGameAction` union                                                       |
33	| `src/types/game-events.ts`                          | Add `expected` field to `GameEvaluateEvent`                                                        |
34	| `src/components/answer-game/answer-game-reducer.ts` | Handle `REJECT_TAP` action                                                                         |
35	| `src/components/answer-game/useTileEvaluation.ts`   | Add `expected` to emits; dispatch `REJECT_TAP` for reject-mode wrong on drag; return `{ correct }` |
36	| `src/components/answer-game/useAutoNextSlot.ts`     | Pending queue, pre-validation, `PlaceResult` return type                                           |
37	| `src/components/answer-game/useDraggableTile.ts`    | Pre-validate on click with shake/sound feedback; drag-reject shake                                 |

128	    expect(next.phase).toBe('playing');

133	      type: 'INIT_ROUND',

135	      zones,

145	      zoneIndex: 0,
146	    });

347	- [ ] **Step 3: Implement changes in useTileEvaluation.ts**
348	
349	Update the `TileEvaluation` interface:
350	
351	```typescript
352	export interface TileEvaluation {
353	  placeTile: (
354	    tileId: string,
355	    zoneIndex: number,
356	  ) => { correct: boolean };
357	  typeTile: (value: string, zoneIndex: number) => void;
358	}
359	```
360	
361	Update `placeTile` implementation:
362	
363	```typescript
364	const placeTile = useCallback(
365	  (tileId: string, zoneIndex: number): { correct: boolean } => {
366	    const tile = state.allTiles.find((t) => t.id === tileId);
367	    const zone = state.zones[zoneIndex];
368	    if (!tile || !zone) return { correct: false };
369	
370	    const correct = tile.value === zone.expectedValue;
371	    if (!skin.suppressDefaultSounds) {
372	      playSound(correct ? 'correct' : 'wrong', 0.8);
373	    }
374	
375	    if (!correct && state.config.wrongTileBehavior === 'reject') {
376	      dispatch({ type: 'REJECT_TAP', tileId, zoneIndex });
377	    } else {
378	      dispatch({ type: 'PLACE_TILE', tileId, zoneIndex });
379	    }
380	
381	    getGameEventBus().emit({
382	      type: 'game:evaluate',
383	      gameId: state.config.gameId,
384	      sessionId: '',
385	      profileId: '',
386	      timestamp: Date.now(),
387	      roundIndex: state.roundIndex,
388	      answer: tileId,
389	      correct,
390	      nearMiss: false,
391	      zoneIndex,
392	      expected: zone.expectedValue,
393	    });
394	
395	    return { correct };
396	  },
397	  [state, dispatch, skin],
398	);
399	```
400	
401	Update `typeTile` to add `expected` to its emit:
402	
403	```typescript
404	getGameEventBus().emit({
405	  type: 'game:evaluate',
406	  gameId: state.config.gameId,
407	  sessionId: '',
408	  profileId: '',
409	  timestamp: Date.now(),
410	  roundIndex: state.roundIndex,
411	  answer: value,
412	  correct,
413	  nearMiss: false,
414	  zoneIndex,
415	  expected: zone.expectedValue,
416	});
417	```
418

440	## Task 4: Refactor useAutoNextSlot — pending queue and pre-validation
441	
442	**Files:**
443	
444	- Modify: `src/components/answer-game/useAutoNextSlot.ts`
445	- Test: `src/components/answer-game/useAutoNextSlot.test.tsx`
446	
447	This is the core fix. Changes:
448	
449	1. Module-scoped `pendingPlacements` array (write-ahead log)
450	2. `PlaceResult` return type: `{ placed: boolean; zoneIndex: number; rejected: boolean }`
451	3. Pre-validation against target slot's `expectedValue` (skipped for `lock-manual`)
452	4. Drain logic removes entries the reducer has applied
453	5. Clear on round transitions via `useEffect`
454	6. Remove `isWrong` early-return guard (superseded by pre-validation)
455	
456	- [ ] **Step 1: Write failing tests**
457

758	Run: `npx vitest run src/components/answer-game/useAutoNextSlot.test.tsx`

762	- [ ] **Step 3: Implement the refactored useAutoNextSlot**
763	
764	Replace the entire content of `src/components/answer-game/useAutoNextSlot.ts`:
765	
766	```typescript
767	import { useCallback, useEffect } from 'react';
768	import { useAnswerGameContext } from './useAnswerGameContext';
769	import { useTileEvaluation } from './useTileEvaluation';
770	
771	export interface PlaceResult {
772	  placed: boolean;
773	  zoneIndex: number;
774	  rejected: boolean;
775	}
776	
777	export interface AutoNextSlot {
778	  placeInNextSlot: (tileId: string) => PlaceResult;
779	}
780	
781	let pendingPlacements: Array<{ tileId: string; zoneIndex: number }> =
782	  [];
783	
784	export const clearPendingPlacements = (): void => {
785	  pendingPlacements = [];
786	};
787	
788	export const useAutoNextSlot = (): AutoNextSlot => {
789	  const state = useAnswerGameContext();
790	  const { zones, activeSlotIndex, config, allTiles, roundIndex } =
791	    state;
792	  const { placeTile } = useTileEvaluation();
793	
794	  useEffect(() => {
795	    clearPendingPlacements();
796	  }, [roundIndex]);
797	
798	  const placeInNextSlot = useCallback(
799	    (tileId: string): PlaceResult => {
800	      pendingPlacements = pendingPlacements.filter(
801	        (entry) =>
802	          zones[entry.zoneIndex]?.placedTileId !== entry.tileId,
803	      );
804	
805	      const claimedZones = new Set(
806	        pendingPlacements.map((e) => e.zoneIndex),
807	      );
808	
809	      const isAvailable = (i: number) =>
810	        zones[i] &&
811	        !zones[i].placedTileId &&
812	        !zones[i].isLocked &&
813	        !claimedZones.has(i);
814	
815	      let targetIndex = -1;
816	      for (let i = activeSlotIndex; i < zones.length; i++) {
817	        if (isAvailable(i)) {
818	          targetIndex = i;
819	          break;
820	        }
821	      }
822	      if (targetIndex === -1) {
823	        for (let i = 0; i < activeSlotIndex; i++) {
824	          if (isAvailable(i)) {
825	            targetIndex = i;
826	            break;
827	          }
828	        }
829	      }
830	
831	      if (targetIndex === -1) {
832	        return { placed: false, zoneIndex: -1, rejected: false };
833	      }
834	
835	      if (config.wrongTileBehavior !== 'lock-manual') {
836	        const tile = allTiles.find((t) => t.id === tileId);
837	        if (tile && tile.value !== zones[targetIndex].expectedValue) {
838	          return {
839	            placed: false,
840	            zoneIndex: targetIndex,
841	            rejected: true,
842	          };
843	        }
844	      }
845	
846	      pendingPlacements.push({ tileId, zoneIndex: targetIndex });
847	      placeTile(tileId, targetIndex);
848	      return { placed: true, zoneIndex: targetIndex, rejected: false };
849	    },
850	    [
851	      zones,
852	      activeSlotIndex,
853	      config.wrongTileBehavior,
854	      allTiles,
855	      placeTile,
856	    ],
857	  );
858	
859	  return { placeInNextSlot };
860	};
861	```
862

1251	Add the drag-reject test:
1252	
1253	```typescript
1254	describe('touch drag reject path', () => {
1255	  it('calls triggerShake on bank tile when drag-drop is rejected in reject mode', () => {
1256	    mockWrongTileBehavior = 'reject';
1257	    mockPlaceTile.mockReturnValue({ correct: false });
1258	
1259	    const tile = { id: 't2', label: 'X', value: 'X' };
1260	    const { result } = renderHook(() => useDraggableTile(tile));
1261	
1262	    const button = document.createElement('button');
1263	    Object.defineProperty(result.current.ref, 'current', {
1264	      value: button,
1265	      writable: true,
1266	    });
1267	
1268	    const onDrop = capturedTouchDragOptions.onDrop as (
1269	      tileId: string,
1270	      zoneIndex: number,
1271	    ) => void;
1272	    act(() => onDrop('t2', 0));
1273	
1274	    expect(mockTriggerShake).toHaveBeenCalledWith(button);
1275	
1276	    mockWrongTileBehavior = 'lock-auto-eject';
1277	  });
1278	
1279	  it('does not shake when wrong tile is dropped in lock-auto-eject mode', () => {
1280	    mockPlaceTile.mockReturnValue({ correct: false });
1281	
1282	    const tile = { id: 't2', label: 'X', value: 'X' };
1283	    renderHook(() => useDraggableTile(tile));
1284	
1285	    const onDrop = capturedTouchDragOptions.onDrop as (
1286	      tileId: string,
1287	      zoneIndex: number,
1288	    ) => void;
1289	    act(() => onDrop('t2', 0));
1290	
1291	    expect(mockTriggerShake).not.toHaveBeenCalled();
1292	  });
1293	});
1294	```
1295	
1296	> **Note:** The `capturedTouchDragOptions` approach captures the callbacks
1297	> `useDraggableTile` passes to `useTouchDrag`. The `stateRef` used in
1298	> `onDrop` (line 29 of useDraggableTile.ts: `const stateRef = useRef(state)`)
1299	> ensures `wrongTileBehavior` reads the current value even inside a stale
1300	> closure. The `requestAnimationFrame` wrapper in the implementation defers
1301	> the shake until after React re-renders the tile at full opacity.

1392	Verify against the spec's behavior matrix:
1393	
1394	| Mode              | Input                      | Expected behavior                                   |
1395	| ----------------- | -------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------- |
1396	| `lock-auto-eject` | Tap wrong tile             | Tile shakes in bank, wrong sound, no slot placement |
1397	| `lock-auto-eject` | Tap correct tile           | Tile placed in slot, correct sound                  |
1398	| `lock-auto-eject` | Rapid correct taps (C-A-T) | All three placed correctly, no false bounces        |
1399	| `lock-manual`     | Tap wrong tile             | Tile goes to slot (unchanged behavior)              |
1400	| `reject`          | Tap wrong tile             | Tile shakes in bank, wrong sound                    |
1401	| `reject`          | Drag wrong tile            | Tile returns to bank, wrong sound, shake            |
1402	| `lock-auto-eject` | Drag wrong tile            | Tile lands in slot, auto-ejects (unchanged)         |

 `./worktrees/276-fast-tap-queue/docs/superpowers/plans/2026-05-01-fast-tap-queue.md`

```text
1	# Fast-Tap Queue Implementation Plan
2	
3	> **For agentic workers:** REQUIRED SUB-SKILL: Use superpowers:subagent-driven-development (recommended) or superpowers:executing-plans to implement this plan task-by-task. Steps use checkbox (`- [ ]`) syntax for tracking.
4	
5	**Goal:** Fix false error bounces on rapid Android taps by pre-validating tiles at the bank and using a ref-based pending-placements queue to prevent stale-closure races.
6	
7	**Architecture:** A module-scoped `pendingPlacements` ref acts as a write-ahead log so concurrent taps see each other's claimed slots without waiting for React's batched re-render. Wrong tiles are caught at the bank (pre-validation) instead of traveling to a slot and bouncing back. A new `REJECT_TAP` reducer action tracks retries without zone mutations.
8	
9	**Tech Stack:** React 19, TypeScript, Vitest, @testing-library/react
10	
11	**Spec:** `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-01-fast-tap-queue-design.md`
12	
13	## Spec Delta
14	
15	**Red flash styling — inline styles instead of CSS class:** The spec says
16	"Add the `.is-wrong` styles to the existing DraggableTile CSS module." However,
17	there is no DraggableTile CSS module — bank tiles use inline styles via
18	`tileStyle()` (which sets `background: linear-gradient(...)`) and Tailwind
19	utility classes. A CSS class cannot override inline styles without `!important`.
20	This plan uses direct `el.style` assignment and `animationend` cleanup instead,
21	achieving the same visual result (skin-wrong-bg/border flash for shake duration)
22	without needing `!important` hacks or a new CSS module.
23	
24	---
25

347	- [ ] **Step 3: Implement changes in useTileEvaluation.ts**
348	
349	Update the `TileEvaluation` interface:
350	
351	```typescript
352	export interface TileEvaluation {
353	  placeTile: (
354	    tileId: string,
355	    zoneIndex: number,
356	  ) => { correct: boolean };
357	  typeTile: (value: string, zoneIndex: number) => void;
358	}
359	```
360	
361	Update `placeTile` implementation:
362	
363	```typescript
364	const placeTile = useCallback(
365	  (tileId: string, zoneIndex: number): { correct: boolean } => {
366	    const tile = state.allTiles.find((t) => t.id === tileId);
367	    const zone = state.zones[zoneIndex];
368	    if (!tile || !zone) return { correct: false };
369	
370	    const correct = tile.value === zone.expectedValue;
371	    if (!skin.suppressDefaultSounds) {
372	      playSound(correct ? 'correct' : 'wrong', 0.8);
373	    }
374	
375	    if (!correct && state.config.wrongTileBehavior === 'reject') {
376	      dispatch({ type: 'REJECT_TAP', tileId, zoneIndex });
377	    } else {
378	      dispatch({ type: 'PLACE_TILE', tileId, zoneIndex });
379	    }
380	
381	    getGameEventBus().emit({
382	      type: 'game:evaluate',
383	      gameId: state.config.gameId,
384	      sessionId: '',
385	      profileId: '',
386	      timestamp: Date.now(),
387	      roundIndex: state.roundIndex,
388	      answer: tileId,
389	      correct,
390	      nearMiss: false,
391	      zoneIndex,
392	      expected: zone.expectedValue,
393	    });
394	
395	    return { correct };
396	  },
397	  [state, dispatch, skin],
398	);
399	```
400

402	
403	```typescript
404	getGameEventBus().emit({
405	  type: 'game:evaluate',
406	  gameId: state.config.gameId,
407	  sessionId: '',
408	  profileId: '',
409	  timestamp: Date.now(),
410	  roundIndex: state.roundIndex,
411	  answer: value,
412	  correct,
413	  nearMiss: false,
414	  zoneIndex,
415	  expected: zone.expectedValue,
416	});
417	```

440	## Task 4: Refactor useAutoNextSlot — pending queue and pre-validation
441	
442	**Files:**
443	
444	- Modify: `src/components/answer-game/useAutoNextSlot.ts`
445	- Test: `src/components/answer-game/useAutoNextSlot.test.tsx`
446	
447	This is the core fix. Changes:
448	
449	1. Module-scoped `pendingPlacements` array (write-ahead log)
450	2. `PlaceResult` return type: `{ placed: boolean; zoneIndex: number; rejected: boolean }`
451	3. Pre-validation against target slot's `expectedValue` (skipped for `lock-manual`)
452	4. Drain logic removes entries the reducer has applied
453	5. Clear on round transitions via `useEffect`
454	6. Remove `isWrong` early-return guard (superseded by pre-validation)
455

457

459	
460	```typescript
461	import { act, renderHook } from '@testing-library/react';

463	import { AnswerGameProvider } from './AnswerGameProvider';

527	
528	function createWrapper(config: AnswerGameConfig) {
529	  const Wrapper = ({ children }: { children: ReactNode }) => (

536	function useHarness(

548	describe('useAutoNextSlot', () => {
549	  describe('basic placement', () => {
550	    it('places correct tile in activeSlotIndex zone', () => {
551	      const Wrapper = createWrapper(gameConfig);

564	
565	    it('advances activeSlotIndex after correct placement', () => {
566	      const Wrapper = createWrapper(gameConfig);
567	      const { result } = renderHook(() => useHarness(), {
568	        wrapper: Wrapper,
569	      });
570	
571	      expect(result.current.state.activeSlotIndex).toBe(0);
572	      act(() => result.current.placeInNextSlot('t1'));
573	      expect(result.current.state.activeSlotIndex).toBe(1);
574	    });
575	
576	    it('skips locked zones when finding the next available slot', () => {
577	      const lockedZones: AnswerZone[] = [
578	        { ...answerZones[0], isLocked: true },
579	        answerZones[1],
580	        answerZones[2],
581	      ];
582	
583	      const Wrapper = createWrapper(gameConfig);
584	      const { result } = renderHook(
585	        () => useHarness(tileItems, lockedZones),
586	        { wrapper: Wrapper },
587	      );
588	
589	      act(() => result.current.placeInNextSlot('t2'));
590	      expect(result.current.state.zones[0]?.placedTileId).toBeNull();
591	      expect(result.current.state.zones[1]?.placedTileId).toBe('t2');
592	    });
593	  });
594	
595	  describe('pre-validation', () => {
596	    it('rejects wrong tile in lock-auto-eject mode', () => {
597	      const Wrapper = createWrapper(gameConfig);
598	      const { result } = renderHook(() => useHarness(), {
599	        wrapper: Wrapper,
600	      });
601	
602	      let res: PlaceResult | undefined;
603	      act(() => {
604	        res = result.current.placeInNextSlot('t2');
605	      });
606	
607	      expect(res).toEqual({ placed: false, zoneIndex: 0, rejected: true });
608	      expect(result.current.state.zones[0]?.placedTileId).toBeNull();
609	    });
610	
611	    it('rejects wrong tile in reject mode', () => {
612	      const rejectConfig: AnswerGameConfig = {
613	        ...gameConfig,
614	        wrongTileBehavior: 'reject',
615	      };
616	      const Wrapper = createWrapper(rejectConfig);
617	      const { result } = renderHook(() => useHarness(), {
618	        wrapper: Wrapper,
619	      });
620	
621	      let res: PlaceResult | undefined;
622	      act(() => {
623	        res = result.current.placeInNextSlot('t2');
624	      });
625	
626	      expect(res).toEqual({ placed: false, zoneIndex: 0, rejected: true });
627	      expect(result.current.state.zones[0]?.placedTileId).toBeNull();
628	    });
629	
630	    it('allows wrong tile through in lock-manual mode (no pre-validation)', () => {
631	      const manualConfig: AnswerGameConfig = {
632	        ...gameConfig,
633	        wrongTileBehavior: 'lock-manual',
634	      };
635	      const Wrapper = createWrapper(manualConfig);
636	      const { result } = renderHook(() => useHarness(), {
637	        wrapper: Wrapper,
638	      });
639	
640	      let res: PlaceResult | undefined;
641	      act(() => {
642	        res = result.current.placeInNextSlot('t2');
643	      });
644	
645	      expect(res).toEqual({ placed: true, zoneIndex: 0, rejected: false });
646	      expect(result.current.state.zones[0]?.placedTileId).toBe('t2');
647	    });
648	  });
649	
650	  describe('pending placements queue', () => {
651	    it('prevents double-placement on rapid taps (stale closure fix)', () => {
652	      const Wrapper = createWrapper(gameConfig);
653	      const { result } = renderHook(() => useHarness(), {
654	        wrapper: Wrapper,
655	      });
656	
657	      act(() => {
658	        result.current.placeInNextSlot('t1');
659	        result.current.placeInNextSlot('t2');
660	      });
661	
662	      expect(result.current.state.zones[0]?.placedTileId).toBe('t1');
663	      expect(result.current.state.zones[1]?.placedTileId).toBe('t2');
664	    });
665	
666	    it('handles three rapid correct taps', () => {
667	      const Wrapper = createWrapper(gameConfig);
668	      const { result } = renderHook(() => useHarness(), {
669	        wrapper: Wrapper,
670	      });
671	
672	      act(() => {
673	        result.current.placeInNextSlot('t1');
674	        result.current.placeInNextSlot('t2');
675	        result.current.placeInNextSlot('t3');
676	      });
677	
678	      expect(result.current.state.zones[0]?.placedTileId).toBe('t1');
679	      expect(result.current.state.zones[1]?.placedTileId).toBe('t2');
680	      expect(result.current.state.zones[2]?.placedTileId).toBe('t3');
681	    });
682	  });
683	
684	  describe('wrap-around', () => {
685	    it('wraps around to fill earlier empty slots', () => {
686	      const threeZones: AnswerZone[] = [
687	        { ...answerZones[0], placedTileId: null },
688	        { ...answerZones[1], placedTileId: 't2' },
689	        { ...answerZones[2], placedTileId: null },
690	      ];
691	
692	      const Wrapper = createWrapper(gameConfig);
693	
694	      function useHarnessWrap() {
695	        const dispatch = useAnswerGameDispatch();
696	        const state = useAnswerGameContext();
697	        if (state.zones.length === 0) {
698	          dispatch({
699	            type: 'INIT_ROUND',
700	            tiles: tileItems,
701	            zones: threeZones,
702	          });
703	          dispatch({ type: 'PLACE_TILE', tileId: 't2', zoneIndex: 1 });
704	        }
705	        const { placeInNextSlot } = useAutoNextSlot();
706	        return { state, placeInNextSlot };
707	      }
708	
709	      const { result } = renderHook(() => useHarnessWrap(), {
710	        wrapper: Wrapper,
711	      });
712	
713	      act(() => result.current.placeInNextSlot('t3'));
714	      act(() => result.current.placeInNextSlot('t1'));
715	
716	      expect(result.current.state.zones[0]?.placedTileId).toBe('t1');
717	    });
718	  });
719	
720	  describe('isWrong guard removal', () => {
721	    it('places correct tile in next slot even when active slot is wrong (lock-auto-eject drag)', () => {
722	      const wrongZones: AnswerZone[] = [
723	        {
724	          ...answerZones[0],
725	          placedTileId: 'tX',
726	          isWrong: true,
727	          isLocked: true,
728	        },
729	        answerZones[1],
730	        answerZones[2],
731	      ];
732	
733	      const tilesWithX: TileItem[] = [
734	        ...tileItems,
735	        { id: 'tX', label: 'X', value: 'X' },
736	      ];
737	
738	      const Wrapper = createWrapper(gameConfig);
739	      const { result } = renderHook(
740	        () => useHarness(tilesWithX, wrongZones),
741	        { wrapper: Wrapper },
742	      );
743	
744	      let res: PlaceResult | undefined;
745	      act(() => {
746	        res = result.current.placeInNextSlot('t2');
747	      });
748	
749	      expect(res).toEqual({ placed: true, zoneIndex: 1, rejected: false });
750	      expect(result.current.state.zones[1]?.placedTileId).toBe('t2');
751	    });
752	  });
753	});
754	```

758	Run: `npx vitest run src/components/answer-game/useAutoNextSlot.test.tsx`

760	Expected: FAIL — `clearPendingPlacements` not exported, `PlaceResult` not exported, `placeInNextSlot` returns `void`, no pre-validation, no pending queue.
761	
762	- [ ] **Step 3: Implement the refactored useAutoNextSlot**
763	
764	Replace the entire content of `src/components/answer-game/useAutoNextSlot.ts`:
765	
766	```typescript
767	import { useCallback, useEffect } from 'react';
768	import { useAnswerGameContext } from './useAnswerGameContext';
769	import { useTileEvaluation } from './useTileEvaluation';
770	
771	export interface PlaceResult {
772	  placed: boolean;
773	  zoneIndex: number;
774	  rejected: boolean;
775	}
776	
777	export interface AutoNextSlot {
778	  placeInNextSlot: (tileId: string) => PlaceResult;
779	}
780	
781	let pendingPlacements: Array<{ tileId: string; zoneIndex: number }> =
782	  [];
783	
784	export const clearPendingPlacements = (): void => {
785	  pendingPlacements = [];
786	};
787	
788	export const useAutoNextSlot = (): AutoNextSlot => {
789	  const state = useAnswerGameContext();
790	  const { zones, activeSlotIndex, config, allTiles, roundIndex } =
791	    state;
792	  const { placeTile } = useTileEvaluation();
793	
794	  useEffect(() => {
795	    clearPendingPlacements();
796	  }, [roundIndex]);
797	
798	  const placeInNextSlot = useCallback(
799	    (tileId: string): PlaceResult => {
800	      pendingPlacements = pendingPlacements.filter(
801	        (entry) =>
802	          zones[entry.zoneIndex]?.placedTileId !== entry.tileId,
803	      );
804	
805	      const claimedZones = new Set(
806	        pendingPlacements.map((e) => e.zoneIndex),
807	      );
808	
809	      const isAvailable = (i: number) =>
810	        zones[i] &&
811	        !zones[i].placedTileId &&
812	        !zones[i].isLocked &&
813	        !claimedZones.has(i);
814	
815	      let targetIndex = -1;
816	      for (let i = activeSlotIndex; i < zones.length; i++) {
817	        if (isAvailable(i)) {
818	          targetIndex = i;
819	          break;
820	        }
821	      }
822	      if (targetIndex === -1) {
823	        for (let i = 0; i < activeSlotIndex; i++) {
824	          if (isAvailable(i)) {
825	            targetIndex = i;
826	            break;
827	          }
828	        }
829	      }
830	
831	      if (targetIndex === -1) {
832	        return { placed: false, zoneIndex: -1, rejected: false };
833	      }
834	
835	      if (config.wrongTileBehavior !== 'lock-manual') {
836	        const tile = allTiles.find((t) => t.id === tileId);
837	        if (tile && tile.value !== zones[targetIndex].expectedValue) {
838	          return {
839	            placed: false,
840	            zoneIndex: targetIndex,
841	            rejected: true,
842	          };
843	        }
844	      }
845	
846	      pendingPlacements.push({ tileId, zoneIndex: targetIndex });
847	      placeTile(tileId, targetIndex);
848	      return { placed: true, zoneIndex: targetIndex, rejected: false };
849	    },
850	    [
851	      zones,
852	      activeSlotIndex,
853	      config.wrongTileBehavior,
854	      allTiles,
855	      placeTile,
856	    ],
857	  );
858	
859	  return { placeInNextSlot };
860	};
861	```
862

1115	Replace the `handleClick` function (lines 129-132):

1117	```typescript
1118	const handleClick = () => {
1119	  if (ref.current?.classList.contains('animate-shake')) return;
1120	
1121	  speakTile(tile.label);
1122	  const result = placeInNextSlot(tile.id);
1123	
1124	  if (result.rejected && ref.current) {
1125	    triggerShake(ref.current);
1126	    const el = ref.current;
1127	    el.style.background = 'var(--skin-wrong-bg)';
1128	    el.style.borderColor = 'var(--skin-wrong-border)';
1129	    el.addEventListener(
1130	      'animationend',
1131	      () => {
1132	        el.style.background = '';
1133	        el.style.borderColor = '';
1134	      },
1135	      { once: true },
1136	    );
1137	
1138	    if (!skin.suppressDefaultSounds) {
1139	      playSound('wrong', 0.8);
1140	    }
1141	
1142	    dispatch({
1143	      type: 'REJECT_TAP',
1144	      tileId: tile.id,
1145	      zoneIndex: result.zoneIndex,
1146	    });
1147	
1148	    getGameEventBus().emit({
1149	      type: 'game:evaluate',
1150	      gameId: state.config.gameId,
1151	      sessionId: '',
1152	      profileId: '',
1153	      timestamp: Date.now(),
1154	      roundIndex: state.roundIndex,
1155	      answer: tile.id,
1156	      correct: false,
1157	      nearMiss: false,
1158	      zoneIndex: result.zoneIndex,
1159	      expected: state.zones[result.zoneIndex]?.expectedValue ?? '',
1160	    });
1161	  }
1162	};
1163	```

1189	- [ ] **Step 1: Write failing test**
1190	
1191	First, add a mock for `useTouchDrag` at module scope (alongside the other mocks
1192	at the top of `useDraggableTile.test.tsx`). This captures the `onDrop` callback

1198	  useTouchDrag: (options: Record<string, unknown>) => {

1206	  },

1210	Then update the `useAnswerGameContext` mock to accept a config override for

1212	module-scope variable:
1213	
1214	```typescript
1215	let mockWrongTileBehavior = 'lock-auto-eject';
1216	
1217	vi.mock('./useAnswerGameContext', () => ({
1218	  useAnswerGameContext: () => ({
1219	    config: {
1220	      inputMethod: 'drag',
1221	      ttsEnabled: true,
1222	      totalRounds: 1,
1223	      gameId: 'test',
1224	      wrongTileBehavior: mockWrongTileBehavior,
1225	      tileBankMode: 'exact',
1226	    },
1227	    zones: [
1228	      {
1229	        id: 'z0',
1230	        index: 0,
1231	        expectedValue: 'C',
1232	        placedTileId: null,
1233	        isWrong: false,

1235	      },
1236	    ],

1247	  }),
1248	}));

1254	describe('touch drag reject path', () => {
1255	  it('calls triggerShake on bank tile when drag-drop is rejected in reject mode', () => {
1256	    mockWrongTileBehavior = 'reject';
1257	    mockPlaceTile.mockReturnValue({ correct: false });
1258	
1259	    const tile = { id: 't2', label: 'X', value: 'X' };
1260	    const { result } = renderHook(() => useDraggableTile(tile));
1261	
1262	    const button = document.createElement('button');
1263	    Object.defineProperty(result.current.ref, 'current', {
1264	      value: button,
1265	      writable: true,
1266	    });
1267	
1268	    const onDrop = capturedTouchDragOptions.onDrop as (
1269	      tileId: string,
1270	      zoneIndex: number,
1271	    ) => void;

1296	> **Note:** The `capturedTouchDragOptions` approach captures the callbacks
1297	> `useDraggableTile` passes to `useTouchDrag`. The `stateRef` used in
1298	> `onDrop` (line 29 of useDraggableTile.ts: `const stateRef = useRef(state)`)
1299	> ensures `wrongTileBehavior` reads the current value even inside a stale
1300	> closure. The `requestAnimationFrame` wrapper in the implementation defers
1301	> the shake until after React re-renders the tile at full opacity.

1	export interface AnswerGameConfig {
2	  gameId: string;
3	  /** @default 'drag' */
4	  inputMethod: 'drag' | 'type' | 'both';
5	  /** @default 'lock-auto-eject' */
6	  wrongTileBehavior: 'reject' | 'lock-manual' | 'lock-auto-eject';
7	  tileBankMode: 'exact' | 'distractors';
8	  distractorCount?: number;
9	  /** Total number of rounds in this session */
10	  totalRounds: number;
11	  /**
12	   * When `true`, rounds follow the order of `rounds` in config.
13	   * When `false` or omitted, order is shuffled once per session (again after Play again).
14	   */
15	  roundsInOrder?: boolean;
16	  /** Whether TTS is enabled for this profile */
17	  ttsEnabled: boolean;
18	  /**
19	   * Controls the OS keyboard type shown on touch devices in type/both mode.
20	   * @default 'text'
21	   */
22	  touchKeyboardInputMode?: 'text' | 'numeric' | 'none';
23	  /** Skin ID for visual variant. Falls back to 'classic' if not registered. */
24	  skin?: string;
25	  /**
26	   * Per-config timing overrides. Take precedence over the skin's own
27	   * `timing` values and baseskill defaults.
28	   */
29	  timing?: {
30	    roundAdvanceDelay?: number;
31	    autoEjectDelay?: number;
32	    levelCompleteDelay?: number;
33	  };
34	  /** When set, `AnswerGameProvider` dispatches `INIT_ROUND` on mount / game change */
35	  initialTiles?: TileItem[];
36	  initialZones?: AnswerZone[];
37	  /** @default inferred: 'free-swap' for drag/both, 'ordered' for type */
38	  slotInteraction?: 'ordered' | 'free-swap';
39	  /** Level progression — omit for classic rounds-only mode */
40	  levelMode?: {
41	    /** Max levels. Omit or set to 0 for unlimited. */
42	    maxLevels?: number;
43	    /**
44	     * Called when the player completes a level to generate the next one.
45	     * Receives the 0-based index of the just-completed level.
46	     * Returns new tiles + zones, or null to end the game early.
47	     */
48	    generateNextLevel: (completedLevel: number) => {
49	      tiles: TileItem[];
50	      zones: AnswerZone[];
51	    } | null;
52	  };
53	  /** Controls which HUD elements render. Each flag is independent. */
54	  hud?: HudConfig;
55	}
56	
57	export interface TileItem {
58	  id: string;
59	  /** Display label shown on the tile (e.g. "A", "cat", "3") */
60	  label: string;
61	  /** Semantic value used for evaluation — matches `AnswerZone.expectedValue` */
62	  value: string;
63	}
64	
65	export interface AnswerZone {
66	  id: string;
67	  index: number;
68	  /** Correct tile value for this slot; matched against `TileItem.value` */
69	  expectedValue: string;
70	  placedTileId: string | null;
71	  isWrong: boolean;
72	  isLocked: boolean;
73	}
74	
75	export type AnswerGamePhase =
76	  | 'playing'
77	  | 'round-complete'
78	  | 'level-complete'
79	  | 'game-over';
80	
81	export interface AnswerGameState {
82	  config: AnswerGameConfig;
83	  /** Full tile list for the current round — never mutated mid-round */
84	  allTiles: TileItem[];
85	  /** IDs of tiles currently visible in the choice bank */
86	  bankTileIds: string[];
87	  zones: AnswerZone[];
88	  /** Index of next slot to fill in auto-next-slot mode */
89	  activeSlotIndex: number;
90	  dragActiveTileId: string | null;
91	  dragHoverZoneIndex: number | null;
92	  /** ID of the bank tile hole currently hovered by a dragged slot tile. */
93	  dragHoverBankTileId: string | null;
94	  phase: AnswerGamePhase;
95	  roundIndex: number;
96	  retryCount: number;
97	  levelIndex: number;
98	  isLevelMode: boolean;
99	}
100	
101	export type AnswerGameAction =
102	  | { type: 'INIT_ROUND'; tiles: TileItem[]; zones: AnswerZone[] }
103	  | { type: 'RESUME_ROUND'; draft: AnswerGameDraftState }
104	  | { type: 'PLACE_TILE'; tileId: string; zoneIndex: number }
105	  | {
106	      type: 'TYPE_TILE';
107	      tileId: string;
108	      value: string;

110	    }
111	  | { type: 'REMOVE_TILE'; zoneIndex: number }
112	  | { type: 'SWAP_TILES'; fromZoneIndex: number; toZoneIndex: number }
113	  | { type: 'EJECT_TILE'; zoneIndex: number }
114	  | { type: 'ADVANCE_ROUND'; tiles: TileItem[]; zones: AnswerZone[] }
115	  | { type: 'ADVANCE_LEVEL'; tiles: TileItem[]; zones: AnswerZone[] }
116	  | { type: 'COMPLETE_GAME' }
117	  | { type: 'SET_DRAG_ACTIVE'; tileId: string | null }
118	  | { type: 'SET_DRAG_HOVER'; zoneIndex: number | null }
119	  | { type: 'SET_DRAG_HOVER_BANK'; tileId: string | null }
120	  | { type: 'SWAP_SLOT_BANK'; zoneIndex: number; bankTileId: string }
121	  | { type: 'SET_ACTIVE_SLOT'; zoneIndex: number };
122	
123	/**
124	 * Snapshot of AnswerGameState persisted to session_history_index.draftState.
125	 * Excludes config (reconstructable from initialContent/initialState),
126	 * dragActiveTileId (transient), and 'game-over' phase (cleared on completion).
127	 */
128	export interface AnswerGameDraftState {
129	  allTiles: TileItem[];
130	  bankTileIds: string[];
131	  zones: AnswerZone[];
132	  activeSlotIndex: number;
133	  phase: 'playing' | 'round-complete' | 'level-complete';
134	  roundIndex: number;
135	  retryCount: number;
136	  levelIndex: number;
137	}
138	
139	export interface HudConfig {
140	  /** Bullet row, one dot per round (classic) or per level (level mode). */
141	  showDots?: boolean;
142	  /** "3/5" label; auto-hides when totalRounds is null. */
143	  showFraction?: boolean;
144	  /** "LEVEL N" label; most useful with level mode. */
145	  showLevel?: boolean;
146	}
147	
148	export interface ProgressHUDProps {
149	  /** 0-based index of the current round. */
150	  roundIndex: number;
151	  /** Total rounds in the session; null for unbounded level mode. */
152	  totalRounds: number | null;
153	  /** 0-based index of the current level; 0 when levelMode is off. */
154	  levelIndex: number;
155	  /** Whether the game uses levelMode. */
156	  isLevelMode: boolean;
157	  /** Lifecycle phase — enables pop/celebration animations on transition. */
158	  phase: AnswerGamePhase;
159	  /** Resolved visibility flags after merging defaults + config. */
160	  showDots: boolean;
161	  showFraction: boolean;
162	  showLevel: boolean;
163	}
