 `./worktrees/276-fast-tap-queue/src/components/answer-game/types.ts`

```text
1	export interface AnswerGameConfig {
2	  gameId: string;
3	  /** @default 'drag' */
4	  inputMethod: 'drag' | 'type' | 'both';
5	  /** @default 'lock-auto-eject' */
6	  wrongTileBehavior: 'reject' | 'lock-manual' | 'lock-auto-eject';
7	  tileBankMode: 'exact' | 'distractors';
8	  distractorCount?: number;
9	  /** Total number of rounds in this session */
10	  totalRounds: number;
11	  /**
12	   * When `true`, rounds follow the order of `rounds` in config.
13	   * When `false` or omitted, order is shuffled once per session (again after Play again).
14	   */
15	  roundsInOrder?: boolean;
16	  /** Whether TTS is enabled for this profile */
17	  ttsEnabled: boolean;
18	  /**
19	   * Controls the OS keyboard type shown on touch devices in type/both mode.
20	   * @default 'text'
21	   */
22	  touchKeyboardInputMode?: 'text' | 'numeric' | 'none';
23	  /** Skin ID for visual variant. Falls back to 'classic' if not registered. */
24	  skin?: string;
25	  /**
26	   * Per-config timing overrides. Take precedence over the skin's own
27	   * `timing` values and baseskill defaults.
28	   */
29	  timing?: {
30	    roundAdvanceDelay?: number;
31	    autoEjectDelay?: number;
32	    levelCompleteDelay?: number;
33	  };
34	  /** When set, `AnswerGameProvider` dispatches `INIT_ROUND` on mount / game change */
35	  initialTiles?: TileItem[];
36	  initialZones?: AnswerZone[];
37	  /** @default inferred: 'free-swap' for drag/both, 'ordered' for type */
38	  slotInteraction?: 'ordered' | 'free-swap';
39	  /** Level progression — omit for classic rounds-only mode */
40	  levelMode?: {
41	    /** Max levels. Omit or set to 0 for unlimited. */
42	    maxLevels?: number;
43	    /**
44	     * Called when the player completes a level to generate the next one.
45	     * Receives the 0-based index of the just-completed level.
46	     * Returns new tiles + zones, or null to end the game early.
47	     */
48	    generateNextLevel: (completedLevel: number) => {
49	      tiles: TileItem[];
50	      zones: AnswerZone[];
51	    } | null;
52	  };
53	  /** Controls which HUD elements render. Each flag is independent. */
54	  hud?: HudConfig;
55	}
56	
57	export interface TileItem {
58	  id: string;
59	  /** Display label shown on the tile (e.g. "A", "cat", "3") */
60	  label: string;
61	  /** Semantic value used for evaluation — matches `AnswerZone.expectedValue` */
62	  value: string;
63	}
64	
65	export interface AnswerZone {
66	  id: string;
67	  index: number;
68	  /** Correct tile value for this slot; matched against `TileItem.value` */
69	  expectedValue: string;
70	  placedTileId: string | null;
71	  isWrong: boolean;
72	  isLocked: boolean;
73	}
74	
75	export type AnswerGamePhase =
76	  | 'playing'
77	  | 'round-complete'
78	  | 'level-complete'
79	  | 'game-over';
80	
81	export interface AnswerGameState {
82	  config: AnswerGameConfig;
83	  /** Full tile list for the current round — never mutated mid-round */
84	  allTiles: TileItem[];
85	  /** IDs of tiles currently visible in the choice bank */
86	  bankTileIds: string[];
87	  zones: AnswerZone[];
88	  /** Index of next slot to fill in auto-next-slot mode */
89	  activeSlotIndex: number;
90	  dragActiveTileId: string | null;
91	  dragHoverZoneIndex: number | null;
92	  /** ID of the bank tile hole currently hovered by a dragged slot tile. */
93	  dragHoverBankTileId: string | null;
94	  phase: AnswerGamePhase;
95	  roundIndex: number;
96	  retryCount: number;
97	  levelIndex: number;
98	  isLevelMode: boolean;
99	}
100	
101	export type AnswerGameAction =
102	  | { type: 'INIT_ROUND'; tiles: TileItem[]; zones: AnswerZone[] }
103	  | { type: 'RESUME_ROUND'; draft: AnswerGameDraftState }
104	  | { type: 'PLACE_TILE'; tileId: string; zoneIndex: number }
105	  | {
106	      type: 'TYPE_TILE';
107	      tileId: string;
108	      value: string;
109	      zoneIndex: number;
110	    }
111	  | { type: 'REMOVE_TILE'; zoneIndex: number }
112	  | { type: 'SWAP_TILES'; fromZoneIndex: number; toZoneIndex: number }
113	  | { type: 'EJECT_TILE'; zoneIndex: number }
114	  | { type: 'ADVANCE_ROUND'; tiles: TileItem[]; zones: AnswerZone[] }
115	  | { type: 'ADVANCE_LEVEL'; tiles: TileItem[]; zones: AnswerZone[] }
116	  | { type: 'COMPLETE_GAME' }
117	  | { type: 'SET_DRAG_ACTIVE'; tileId: string | null }
118	  | { type: 'SET_DRAG_HOVER'; zoneIndex: number | null }
119	  | { type: 'SET_DRAG_HOVER_BANK'; tileId: string | null }
120	  | { type: 'SWAP_SLOT_BANK'; zoneIndex: number; bankTileId: string }
121	  | { type: 'SET_ACTIVE_SLOT'; zoneIndex: number };
122	
123	/**
124	 * Snapshot of AnswerGameState persisted to session_history_index.draftState.
125	 * Excludes config (reconstructable from initialContent/initialState),
126	 * dragActiveTileId (transient), and 'game-over' phase (cleared on completion).
127	 */
128	export interface AnswerGameDraftState {
129	  allTiles: TileItem[];
130	  bankTileIds: string[];
131	  zones: AnswerZone[];
132	  activeSlotIndex: number;
133	  phase: 'playing' | 'round-complete' | 'level-complete';
134	  roundIndex: number;
135	  retryCount: number;
136	  levelIndex: number;
137	}
138	
139	export interface HudConfig {
140	  /** Bullet row, one dot per round (classic) or per level (level mode). */
141	  showDots?: boolean;
142	  /** "3/5" label; auto-hides when totalRounds is null. */
143	  showFraction?: boolean;
144	  /** "LEVEL N" label; most useful with level mode. */
145	  showLevel?: boolean;
146	}
147	
148	export interface ProgressHUDProps {
149	  /** 0-based index of the current round. */
150	  roundIndex: number;
151	  /** Total rounds in the session; null for unbounded level mode. */
152	  totalRounds: number | null;
153	  /** 0-based index of the current level; 0 when levelMode is off. */
154	  levelIndex: number;
155	  /** Whether the game uses levelMode. */
156	  isLevelMode: boolean;
157	  /** Lifecycle phase — enables pop/celebration animations on transition. */
158	  phase: AnswerGamePhase;
159	  /** Resolved visibility flags after merging defaults + config. */
160	  showDots: boolean;
161	  showFraction: boolean;
162	  showLevel: boolean;
163	}
164
```
</details></tool-use>

1	/** Aligned with docs/game-engine.md §3 (expand in M4 as needed). */
2	export type GradeBand =
3	  | 'pre-k'
4	  | 'k'
5	  | 'year1-2'
6	  | 'year3-4'
7	  | 'year5-6';
8	
9	export type GameEventType =
10	  | 'game:start'
11	  | 'game:instructions_shown'
12	  | 'game:action'
13	  | 'game:evaluate'
14	  | 'game:score'
15	  | 'game:hint'
16	  | 'game:retry'
17	  | 'game:time_up'
18	  | 'game:end'
19	  | 'game:round-advance'
20	  | 'game:level-advance'
21	  | 'game:drag-start'
22	  | 'game:drag-over-zone'
23	  | 'game:tile-ejected';
24	
25	export interface BaseGameEvent {
26	  type: GameEventType;
27	  gameId: string;
28	  sessionId: string;
29	  profileId: string;
30	  timestamp: number;
31	  roundIndex: number;
32	}
33	
34	export interface GameStartEvent extends BaseGameEvent {
35	  type: 'game:start';
36	  locale: string;
37	  difficulty: string;
38	  gradeBand: GradeBand;
39	}
40	
41	export interface GameInstructionsShownEvent extends BaseGameEvent {
42	  type: 'game:instructions_shown';
43	}
44	
45	export interface GameActionEvent extends BaseGameEvent {
46	  type: 'game:action';
47	  actionType: string;
48	  payload: Record<string, string | number | boolean>;
49	}
50	
51	export interface GameEvaluateEvent extends BaseGameEvent {
52	  type: 'game:evaluate';
53	  answer: string | string[] | number;
54	  correct: boolean;
55	  nearMiss: boolean;
56	  /** Index of the slot the tile was placed into. */
57	  zoneIndex: number;
58	}
59	
60	export interface GameScoreEvent extends BaseGameEvent {
61	  type: 'game:score';
62	  pointsAwarded: number;
63	  totalScore: number;
64	  streak: number;
65	}
66	
67	export interface GameHintEvent extends BaseGameEvent {
68	  type: 'game:hint';
69	  hintType: string;
70	}
71	
72	export interface GameRetryEvent extends BaseGameEvent {
73	  type: 'game:retry';
74	  retryCount: number;
75	}
76	
77	export interface GameTimeUpEvent extends BaseGameEvent {
78	  type: 'game:time_up';
79	}
80	
81	export interface GameEndEvent extends BaseGameEvent {
82	  type: 'game:end';
83	  finalScore: number;
84	  totalRounds: number;
85	  correctCount: number;
86	  durationMs: number;
87	  retryCount: number;
88	}
89	
90	export interface GameRoundAdvanceEvent extends BaseGameEvent {
91	  type: 'game:round-advance';
92	}
93	
94	export interface GameLevelAdvanceEvent extends BaseGameEvent {
95	  type: 'game:level-advance';
96	  levelIndex: number;
97	}
98	
99	export interface GameDragStartEvent extends BaseGameEvent {
100	  type: 'game:drag-start';
101	  tileId: string;
102	}
103	
104	export interface GameDragOverZoneEvent extends BaseGameEvent {
105	  type: 'game:drag-over-zone';
106	  zoneIndex: number;
107	}
108	
109	export interface GameTileEjectedEvent extends BaseGameEvent {
110	  type: 'game:tile-ejected';
111	  zoneIndex: number;
112	  tileId: string | null;
113	}
114	
115	export type GameEvent =
116	  | GameStartEvent
117	  | GameInstructionsShownEvent
118	  | GameActionEvent
119	  | GameEvaluateEvent
120	  | GameScoreEvent
121	  | GameHintEvent
122	  | GameRetryEvent
123	  | GameTimeUpEvent
124	  | GameEndEvent
125	  | GameRoundAdvanceEvent
126	  | GameLevelAdvanceEvent
127	  | GameDragStartEvent
128	  | GameDragOverZoneEvent
129	  | GameTileEjectedEvent;
130	
131	export interface GameEventBus {
132	  emit: (event: GameEvent) => void;
133	  subscribe: (
134	    type: GameEventType | 'game:*',
135	    handler: (event: GameEvent) => void,
136	  ) => () => void;
137	}
138

1	import { act, renderHook } from '@testing-library/react';
2	import {
3	  afterEach,
4	  beforeEach,
5	  describe,
6	  expect,
7	  it,
8	  vi,
9	} from 'vitest';
10	import { AnswerGameProvider } from './AnswerGameProvider';
11	import { useAnswerGameContext } from './useAnswerGameContext';
12	import { useAnswerGameDispatch } from './useAnswerGameDispatch';
13	import { useTileEvaluation } from './useTileEvaluation';
14	import type { AnswerGameConfig, AnswerZone, TileItem } from './types';
15	import type { ReactNode } from 'react';
16	import { playSound } from '@/lib/audio/AudioFeedback';
17	import { __resetSkinRegistryForTests, registerSkin } from '@/lib/skin';
18	
19	vi.mock('@/lib/game-event-bus', () => ({
20	  getGameEventBus: () => ({ emit: vi.fn(), subscribe: vi.fn() }),
21	}));
22	
23	vi.mock('@/lib/audio/AudioFeedback', () => ({
24	  playSound: vi.fn(),
25	  queueSound: vi.fn(),
26	  whenSoundEnds: vi.fn().mockImplementation(() => Promise.resolve()),
27	}));
28	
29	const baseConfig: AnswerGameConfig = {
30	  gameId: 'test',
31	  inputMethod: 'drag',
32	  wrongTileBehavior: 'lock-auto-eject',
33	  tileBankMode: 'exact',
34	  totalRounds: 1,
35	  ttsEnabled: false,
36	};
37	
38	const tiles: TileItem[] = [
39	  { id: 't1', label: 'C', value: 'C' },
40	  { id: 't2', label: 'X', value: 'X' },
41	];
42	
43	const zones: AnswerZone[] = [
44	  {
45	    id: 'z0',
46	    index: 0,
47	    expectedValue: 'C',
48	    placedTileId: null,
49	    isWrong: false,
50	    isLocked: false,
51	  },
52	];
53	
54	function createWrapper(config: AnswerGameConfig) {
55	  const Wrapper = ({ children }: { children: ReactNode }) => (
56	    <AnswerGameProvider config={config}>{children}</AnswerGameProvider>
57	  );
58	  Wrapper.displayName = 'AnswerGameTestWrapper';
59	  return Wrapper;
60	}
61	
62	function useTileEvaluationHarness() {
63	  const dispatch = useAnswerGameDispatch();
64	  const state = useAnswerGameContext();
65	  if (state.zones.length === 0) {
66	    dispatch({ type: 'INIT_ROUND', tiles, zones });
67	  }
68	  const { placeTile } = useTileEvaluation();
69	  return { state, placeTile };
70	}
71	
72	function useInitialisedEvaluation(_config: AnswerGameConfig) {
73	  const dispatch = useAnswerGameDispatch();
74	  const { zones: stateZones } = useAnswerGameContext();
75	  if (stateZones.length === 0) {
76	    dispatch({ type: 'INIT_ROUND', tiles, zones });
77	  }
78	  return useTileEvaluation();
79	}
80	
81	describe('useTileEvaluation', () => {
82	  beforeEach(() => {
83	    vi.useFakeTimers();
84	    vi.clearAllMocks();
85	  });
86	  afterEach(() => vi.useRealTimers());
87	
88	  it('plays "correct" sound on correct tile placement', () => {
89	    const { result } = renderHook(
90	      () => useInitialisedEvaluation(baseConfig),
91	      { wrapper: createWrapper(baseConfig) },
92	    );
93	    act(() => result.current.placeTile('t1', 0));
94	    expect(playSound).toHaveBeenCalledWith('correct', 0.8);
95	  });
96	
97	  it('plays "wrong" sound on incorrect tile placement', () => {
98	    const { result } = renderHook(() => useTileEvaluationHarness(), {
99	      wrapper: createWrapper(baseConfig),
100	    });
101	    act(() => result.current.placeTile('t2', 0));
102	    expect(playSound).toHaveBeenCalledWith('wrong', 0.8);
103	  });
104	
105	  it('correct placement: zone not marked wrong', () => {
106	    const { result } = renderHook(
107	      () => useInitialisedEvaluation(baseConfig),
108	      {
109	        wrapper: createWrapper(baseConfig),
110	      },
111	    );
112	    act(() => result.current.placeTile('t1', 0));
113	    expect(result.current.placeTile).toBeDefined();
114	  });
115	
116	  it('lock-auto-eject: wrong tile is placed and marked wrong (eject handled by slot)', () => {
117	    const { result } = renderHook(() => useTileEvaluationHarness(), {
118	      wrapper: createWrapper(baseConfig),
119	    });
120	
121	    act(() => result.current.placeTile('t2', 0));
122	    expect(result.current.state.zones[0]?.isWrong).toBe(true);
123	    // EJECT_TILE is now dispatched by useSlotBehavior, not useTileEvaluation,
124	    // so advancing time here should NOT clear the zone.
125	    act(() => vi.advanceTimersByTime(1000));
126	    expect(result.current.state.zones[0]?.isWrong).toBe(true);
127	  });
128	
129	  it('reject: no zone placement, only retryCount incremented', () => {
130	    const rejectConfig: AnswerGameConfig = {
131	      ...baseConfig,
132	      wrongTileBehavior: 'reject',
133	    };
134	
135	    const { result } = renderHook(() => useTileEvaluationHarness(), {
136	      wrapper: createWrapper(rejectConfig),
137	    });
138	
139	    act(() => result.current.placeTile('t2', 0));
140	    expect(result.current.state.zones[0]?.placedTileId).toBeNull();
141	    expect(result.current.state.retryCount).toBe(1);
142	  });
143	
144	  it('lock-manual: wrong tile stays until manually removed', () => {
145	    const manualConfig: AnswerGameConfig = {
146	      ...baseConfig,
147	      wrongTileBehavior: 'lock-manual',
148	    };
149	
150	    const { result } = renderHook(() => useTileEvaluationHarness(), {
151	      wrapper: createWrapper(manualConfig),
152	    });
153	
154	    act(() => result.current.placeTile('t2', 0));
155	    expect(result.current.state.zones[0]?.isWrong).toBe(true);
156	
157	    act(() => vi.advanceTimersByTime(2000));
158	    expect(result.current.state.zones[0]?.placedTileId).toBe('t2');
159	  });
160	
161	  it('skips default sound when skin.suppressDefaultSounds is true', () => {
162	    __resetSkinRegistryForTests();
163	    registerSkin('test', {
164	      id: 'silent',
165	      name: 'Silent',
166	      tokens: {},
167	      suppressDefaultSounds: true,
168	    });
169	
170	    const config: AnswerGameConfig = { ...baseConfig, skin: 'silent' };
171	    const { result } = renderHook(() => useTileEvaluationHarness(), {
172	      wrapper: createWrapper(config),
173	    });
174	
175	    vi.mocked(playSound).mockClear();
176	
177	    act(() => {
178	      result.current.placeTile('t1', 0);
179	    });
180	
181	    expect(playSound).not.toHaveBeenCalled();
182	  });
183	});
184

1	import { describe, expect, it } from 'vitest';
2	import { createGameEventBus } from './game-event-bus';
3	import type { GameEvaluateEvent, GameEvent } from '@/types/game-events';
4	
5	function evaluateEvent(): GameEvaluateEvent {
6	  return {
7	    type: 'game:evaluate',
8	    gameId: 'g1',
9	    sessionId: 's1',
10	    profileId: 'p1',
11	    timestamp: 1,
12	    roundIndex: 0,

15	    nearMiss: false,
16	    zoneIndex: 0,
17	  };
18	}

20	describe('TypedGameEventBus', () => {
21	  it('delivers emit to exact-type subscribers', () => {
22	    const bus = createGameEventBus();
23	    const seen: GameEvent[] = [];
24	    bus.subscribe('game:evaluate', (e) => seen.push(e));
25	    const event_ = evaluateEvent();
26	    bus.emit(event_);
27	    expect(seen).toEqual([event_]);
28	  });
29	
30	  it('delivers all events to game:*', () => {
31	    const bus = createGameEventBus();
32	    const seen: GameEvent[] = [];
33	    bus.subscribe('game:*', (e) => seen.push(e));
34	    const event_ = evaluateEvent();
35	    bus.emit(event_);
36	    expect(seen).toEqual([event_]);
37	  });
38	
39	  it('unsubscribe stops delivery', () => {
40	    const bus = createGameEventBus();
41	    const seen: GameEvent[] = [];
42	    const off = bus.subscribe('game:evaluate', (e) => seen.push(e));
43	    off();
44	    bus.emit(evaluateEvent());
45	    expect(seen).toEqual([]);
46	  });
47	});

_**Agent - sidechain (claude-haiku-4-5-20251001 2026-05-01 07:25:51Z)**_

I've read the complete 1408-line plan document. Now let me conduct a thorough consistency review.

## Key Findings from Analysis:

**1. Terminology inconsistency in return type naming (Task 4 vs Task 5 code samples)**
- Task 4 defines `PlaceResult` with `rejected: boolean` field
- Task 5's test mock (line 903-906) and test assertions (lines 1025-1030, 1051-1056) reference the same `PlaceResult` interface
- The implementation code samples are internally consistent, but there's a **naming ambiguity**: is a "rejected" placement one that failed validation, or one that was explicitly rejected (REJECT_TAP dispatch)? The context makes it clear (failed validation before dispatch), but the field name could cause confusion.

**2. Missing behavioral specification for PlaceResult return when no slot available**
- Line 832 returns `{ placed: false, zoneIndex: -1, rejected: false }` when no target slot is found
- This case is never tested in the provided test file
- Task 5's tests don't cover this edge case (no zone available scenario)

**3. Inconsistency between architecture description and implementation details**
- Line 5-7 (Architecture section) states the queue is "module-scoped" and acts as "write-ahead log"
- Line 7 says "wrong tiles are caught at the bank (pre-validation)"
- But Task 5 implementation (lines 1117-1162) handles rejection feedback only for click reject path, not drag reject path until Task 6
- This creates temporal confusion: when is pre-validation happening for drag vs. click?

**4. Spec Delta statement is vague about CSS class resolution**
- Lines 15-22 claim there is "no DraggableTile CSS module" and say inline styles will be used
- But Task 5 code (line 1127-1128) uses CSS custom properties: `var(--skin-wrong-bg)` and `var(--skin-wrong-border)`
- These variables must be defined somewhere; the plan doesn't specify where (global CSS, Tailwind config, or elsewhere)
- Risk: executor may not know where to define the custom properties

**5. placeTile return type change contradicts earlier implicit usage**
- Task 1 adds `expected` field to `GameEvaluateEvent` (line 79)
- Task 3 changes `placeTile` to return `{ correct: boolean }` (lines 354-356)
- But Task 2's reducer test (lines 112-149) never calls `placeTile` directly
- Task 4's test helper (line 847) calls `placeTile(tileId, targetIndex)` without capturing return value
- Task 5's test (line 1257) mocks `mockPlaceTile.mockReturnValue({ correct: false })`—but this is called from within `useAutoNextSlot`, not exposed to callers
- **Contradiction**: Task 4 implementation calls `placeTile` (line 847) but never uses its return value, yet Task 5 expects it to return `{ correct: boolean }`

**6. Ambiguity in PlaceResult.rejected semantics across modes**
- Line 595: "pre-validation rejects wrong tile in lock-auto-eject mode"
- Line 611: "rejects wrong tile in reject mode"
- Line 630: "allows wrong tile through in lock-manual mode (no pre-validation)"
- But all three modes set `config.wrongTileBehavior`, which only changes behavior in `placeTile` dispatch (Task 3, lines 375-378)
- The pre-validation logic (Task 4, lines 835-844) skips validation only for `'lock-manual'`, meaning lock-auto-eject and reject both pre-validate
- So why do both "reject" the tile? The difference is in what `placeTile` dispatches, but `placeInNextSlot` doesn't call `placeTile` if pre-validation fails
- **Question**: How does `reject` mode differ from `lock-auto-eject` at the `useAutoNextSlot` level if both reject at pre-validation?

**7. Ambiguous ownership of wrong-tile feedback between tasks**
- Task 3 (useTileEvaluation) describes "dispatch REJECT_TAP for reject-mode wrong on drag" (line 198)
- Task 5 (useDraggableTile click) implements click-reject feedback (shake, sound, dispatch REJECT_TAP)
- Task 6 (useDraggableTile drag) implements drag-reject feedback (shake, sound) but delegates to `placeTile`
- But Task 3's implementation of `placeTile` (lines 364-398) **also emits `game:evaluate` for all tiles**
- Task 5's click reject also emits `game:evaluate` (lines 1148-1160)
- **Risk of double-emit**: Click path goes through `placeInNextSlot` → `placeTile` (emits), then `handleClick` also emits on rejection—but only if `result.rejected` is true
- This works because `placeInNextSlot` returns early on validation failure (line 838), so `placeTile` is never called, no duplicate emit
- However, the control flow is indirect and fragile

**8. Drag feedback timing: requestAnimationFrame justification unclear**
- Line 1301 note states `requestAnimationFrame` "defers the shake until after React re-renders the tile at full opacity"
- But Task 6 code (line 1322) wraps the shake in `requestAnimationFrame`, suggesting the tile has been removed from the DOM and re-added
- This timing assumption is never verified in tests; test mocks `onDrop` and invokes it directly without simulating real drag/drop or React re-renders
- **Risk**: If React's re-render timing changes, the assumption breaks

**9. Unresolved external dependency: where are skin CSS variables defined?**
- Lines 1127-1128, 1325-1326 use `var(--skin-wrong-bg)` and `var(--skin-wrong-border)`
- Spec Delta (line 15-22) promises "skin-wrong-bg/border/shake" styling
- But the plan never specifies where these variables are defined (which CSS file, Tailwind config plugin, skin module export)
- Executor will need to discover or create this definition without guidance

**10. Contradictory guidance on pre-validation behavior for lock-manual**
- Line 630: "allows wrong tile through in lock-manual mode (no pre-validation)"
- Task 4 implementation (line 835): `if (config.wrongTileBehavior !== 'lock-manual')` skips validation
- This is consistent, but the plan never explains _why_ lock-manual skips validation
- Is it because lock-manual expects all tiles to go to slots and rely on manual verification? Or for backwards compatibility? The plan states the behavior but not the rationale.

---

 `./worktrees/276-fast-tap-queue/src/components/answer-game/Slot/slot-animations.ts`

```text
1	/** Adds animate-shake class, removes it on animationend. */
2	export const triggerShake = (el: HTMLElement): void => {
3	  el.classList.remove('animate-shake');
4	  // Force reflow to restart animation if already running
5	  void el.offsetWidth;
6	  el.classList.add('animate-shake');
7	  el.addEventListener(
8	    'animationend',
9	    () => el.classList.remove('animate-shake'),
10	    { once: true },
11	  );
12	};
13	
14	/** Adds animate-pop class, removes it on animationend. */
15	export const triggerPop = (el: HTMLElement): void => {
16	  el.classList.remove('animate-pop');
17	  void el.offsetWidth;
18	  el.classList.add('animate-pop');
19	  el.addEventListener(
20	    'animationend',
21	    () => el.classList.remove('animate-pop'),
22	    { once: true },
23	  );
24	};
25	
26	/**
27	 * Animates a tile flying from its slot toward the tile bank.
28	 *
29	 * Phase 1: ghost flies to the hole (transform only, no opacity change).
30	 * Phase 2: ghost fades out — but only when the caller invokes `startFade()`.
31	 *          This lets the caller wait until the real tile has appeared in the
32	 *          bank (i.e. after EJECT_TILE fires) before fading the ghost out,
33	 *          preventing a visible gap between ghost disappearing and tile appearing.
34	 *
35	 * Returns { startFade } — call it when the bank tile is ready to be shown.
36	 * If startFade is called before phase 1 completes, the fade starts immediately
37	 * after the ghost arrives. Calling it multiple times is a no-op.
38	 *
39	 * el is the button inside the slot. We use its parent (the slot li) for
40	 * accurate pixel dimensions and border-radius, then build a fresh fixed-
41	 * position ghost div so the animation escapes overflow:hidden ancestors
42	 * and avoids size-full / percentage class conflicts.
43	 */
44	export const triggerEjectReturn = (
45	  el: HTMLElement,
46	  tileId: string | null,
47	  onComplete: () => void,
48	): { startFade: () => void } => {
49	  // Slot element gives us correct size + border-radius (button has size-full %).
50	  const sourceEl = el.parentElement ?? el;
51	  const sourceRect = sourceEl.getBoundingClientRect();
52	  const { borderRadius } = globalThis.getComputedStyle(sourceEl);
53	
54	  // Fresh div — no inherited classes that could break fixed positioning.
55	  const ghost = document.createElement('div');
56	  ghost.innerHTML = el.innerHTML;
57	  ghost.style.cssText = `
58	    position: fixed;
59	    top: ${sourceRect.top}px;
60	    left: ${sourceRect.left}px;
61	    width: ${sourceRect.width}px;
62	    height: ${sourceRect.height}px;
63	    border-radius: ${borderRadius};
64	    display: flex;
65	    align-items: center;
66	    justify-content: center;
67	    background: var(--card);
68	    box-shadow: 0 4px 16px rgba(0,0,0,0.18);
69	    z-index: 9999;
70	    pointer-events: none;
71	    margin: 0;
72	  `;
73	  document.body.append(ghost);
74	
75	  // Hide the original so only the ghost is visible during the flight.
76	  el.style.opacity = '0';
77	
78	  const cleanup = () => {
79	    ghost.remove();
80	    el.style.opacity = '';
81	    onComplete();
82	  };
83	
84	  // Look up the specific hole for this tile, or fall back to the bank container.
85	  const targetEl = tileId
86	    ? document.querySelector<HTMLElement>(
87	        `[data-tile-bank-hole="${tileId}"]`,
88	      )
89	    : document.querySelector<HTMLElement>('[data-tile-bank]');
90	
91	  // startFade state machine: phase-1 may complete before or after the caller
92	  // triggers the fade, so we track both independently.
93	  let fadeRequested = false;
94	  let phaseOneDone = false;
95	
96	  const doFade = () => {
97	    ghost.style.transition = 'opacity 200ms ease-out';
98	    void ghost.offsetWidth;
99	    ghost.style.opacity = '0';
100	    ghost.addEventListener('transitionend', cleanup, { once: true });
101	  };
102	
103	  const startFade = () => {
104	    if (fadeRequested) return; // idempotent
105	    fadeRequested = true;
106	    if (phaseOneDone) doFade();
107	  };
108	
109	  if (!targetEl) {
110	    // No target — fade in place immediately (no hole to fly to).
111	    ghost.style.transition = 'opacity 300ms ease-in';
112	    void ghost.offsetWidth;
113	    ghost.style.opacity = '0';
114	    ghost.addEventListener('transitionend', cleanup, { once: true });
115	    return { startFade };
116	  }
117	
118	  const bankRect = targetEl.getBoundingClientRect();
119	  const deltaX =
120	    bankRect.left +
121	    bankRect.width / 2 -
122	    (sourceRect.left + sourceRect.width / 2);
123	  const deltaY =
124	    bankRect.top +
125	    bankRect.height / 2 -
126	    (sourceRect.top + sourceRect.height / 2);
127	
128	  // Phase 1: fly to the hole (no opacity change during flight).
129	  ghost.style.transition = 'transform 300ms ease-in';
130	  void ghost.offsetWidth;
131	  ghost.style.transform = `translate(${deltaX}px, ${deltaY}px)`;
132	
133	  ghost.addEventListener(
134	    'transitionend',
135	    () => {
136	      phaseOneDone = true;
137	      if (fadeRequested) doFade();
138	      // Otherwise ghost waits at hole until startFade() is called.
139	    },
140	    { once: true },
141	  );
142	
143	  return { startFade };
144	};
145
```
</details></tool-use>

1	/** Adds animate-shake class, removes it on animationend. */
2	export const triggerShake = (el: HTMLElement): void => {
3	  el.classList.remove('animate-shake');
4	  // Force reflow to restart animation if already running
5	  void el.offsetWidth;
6	  el.classList.add('animate-shake');

45	            className="rounded border px-2 py-1"

364	const placeTile = useCallback(
365	  (tileId: string, zoneIndex: number): { correct: boolean } => {
366	    const tile = state.allTiles.find((t) => t.id === tileId);
367	    const zone = state.zones[zoneIndex];
368	    if (!tile || !zone) return { correct: false };
369

372	      playSound(correct ? 'correct' : 'wrong', 0.8);
373	    }
374	
375	    if (!correct && state.config.wrongTileBehavior === 'reject') {

377	    } else {
378	      dispatch({ type: 'PLACE_TILE', tileId, zoneIndex });

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./worktrees/276-fast-tap-queue/docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-01-fast-tap-queue-design.md`

```text
1	# Fast-Tap Queue & Pre-Validation Design
2	
3	**Issue:** [#276](https://github.com/leocaseiro/base-skill/issues/276)
4	**Date:** 2026-05-01
5	
6	## Problem
7	
8	On Android touch devices, tapping letter tiles quickly in the correct order
9	(e.g. C-A-T within ~100ms) causes false error bounces. React's click handlers
10	fire synchronously, but reducer dispatches are batched — the second tap reads
11	stale `activeSlotIndex` and `zones` from its closure before the first
12	`PLACE_TILE` has flushed.
13	
14	Mouse clicks cannot reproduce the bug (insufficient speed). Drag-and-drop is
15	unaffected (user targets a specific slot).
16	
17	## Solution: Pre-Validation at the Bank + Input Buffer
18	
19	### Pre-validation on tap/click
20	
21	When a bank tile is tapped (not dragged), check the tile's value against the
22	target slot's `expectedValue` **before** dispatching `PLACE_TILE`.
23	Pre-validation reads `expectedValue` from `zones[computedTargetIndex]`, where
24	`computedTargetIndex` is the next empty slot accounting for pending placements
25	in the ref (not the stale `activeSlotIndex` from the closure). Behavior
26	varies by `wrongTileBehavior`:
27	
28	| `wrongTileBehavior` | Tap/click (new)                                          | Drag (unchanged)                                    |
29	| ------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------- |
30	| `lock-auto-eject`   | Pre-validate at bank — wrong tile shakes in bank         | Lands in slot, auto-ejects after delay              |
31	| `lock-manual`       | **No change** — wrong tile goes to slot, stays locked    | Same — no change                                    |
32	| `reject`            | Shake + wrong sound + red flash on bank tile (all input) | Same shake + wrong sound + red flash (new feedback) |
33	
34	For `reject` mode, the feedback applies to **all input methods** (tap, click,
35	and drag). The reducer already silently discards wrong tiles in this mode
36	(lines 119-125 of `answer-game-reducer.ts`); we add visual+audio feedback
37	where there was none.
38	
39	### Input buffer for rapid correct taps
40	
41	A shared ref-based pending-placements queue prevents the stale-closure race.
42	The ref must be shared across all hook instances — either lifted to
43	`AnswerGameProvider` context or stored at module scope — so that concurrent
44	taps from different tile components see each other's pending claims.
45	
46	- Maintain a `pendingPlacements` ref (`Array<{ tileId: string; zoneIndex: number }>`)
47	- When `placeInNextSlot` is called, compute the target slot accounting for
48	  both current `zones` state AND pending placements in the ref
49	- Push the placement into the ref, then dispatch `PLACE_TILE`
50	- Drain condition: on re-render, remove entries where
51	  `zones[entry.zoneIndex].placedTileId === entry.tileId` (the reducer has
52	  applied them)
53	- Clear the ref entirely on `INIT_ROUND`, `ADVANCE_ROUND`, and
54	  `ADVANCE_LEVEL` to prevent stale entries across rounds
55	
56	The ref is synchronous (no React batching delay) — a write-ahead log that
57	lets the second tap see slot 0 is claimed and correctly target slot 1. No
58	`flushSync`, no debounce timers.
59	
60	### Bank tile shake animation
61	
62	`triggerShake` from `slot-animations.ts` is called on the bank tile's
63	`<button>` ref when pre-validation rejects a tap. Same `animate-shake` CSS
64	class (300ms oscillate-X) — no new keyframes.
65	
66	For the red flash: briefly apply a CSS class (`is-wrong`) to the bank tile
67	button for the duration of the shake animation (~300ms), using the existing
68	`--skin-wrong-bg` and `--skin-wrong-border` CSS variables so it matches the
69	slot wrong-tile styling. Remove the class on `animationend`. Add the
70	`.is-wrong` styles to the existing DraggableTile CSS module (co-located with
71	the component) — no new stylesheet needed.
72	
73	**Throttle on rapid re-tap:** If the bank tile already has `animate-shake`
74	(a shake is in progress), ignore subsequent taps. This prevents restarting
75	the animation mid-cycle, which would orphan the `animationend` listener and
76	leave the `is-wrong` class permanently applied.
77	
78	`useAutoNextSlot.placeInNextSlot` returns
79	`{ placed: boolean; zoneIndex: number; rejected: boolean }` instead of
80	`void`, so callers can branch on the outcome.
81	
82	Flow in `useDraggableTile.handleClick`:
83	
84	1. `speakTile(tile.label)` (unchanged)
85	2. Call `placeInNextSlot(tile.id)` — pre-validates tile value against
86	   `zones[computedTargetIndex].expectedValue`
87	3. If `rejected` and not `lock-manual`: `triggerShake(ref.current)`, play
88	   wrong sound, dispatch `REJECT_TAP` with `zoneIndex` from the result
89	4. If `placed`: tile is pushed to pending ref and `PLACE_TILE` dispatched
90	
91	Pre-validation supersedes the existing `isWrong` early-return guard
92	(`if (zones[activeSlotIndex]?.isWrong) return`) for tap/click paths — the
93	tile is checked at the bank before reaching the reducer. The `isWrong` guard
94	remains active for drag and `lock-manual` paths where tiles still land in
95	slots.
96	
97	### New `REJECT_TAP` reducer action
98	
99	```typescript
100	{
101	  type: 'REJECT_TAP';
102	  tileId: string;
103	  zoneIndex: number;
104	}
105	```
106	
107	Increments `retryCount` only. No zone mutations, no bank changes, no
108	`activeSlotIndex` movement.
109	
110	### Add `expected` field to `game:evaluate` events
111	
112	Add `expected: string` to the `GameEvaluateEvent` type, populated from
113	`zone.expectedValue` at all emit sites:
114	
115	- `useTileEvaluation.placeTile`
116	- `useTileEvaluation.typeTile`
117	- Pre-validation reject path in `useDraggableTile.handleClick`
118	
119	Non-breaking additive change. No IndexedDB migration needed — `game:evaluate`
120	events are in-memory only, and session history payloads use
121	`additionalProperties: true`.
122	
123	Provides the confusion pair (`answer` vs `expected`) for future SRS (Anki
124	algorithm) implementation.
125	
126	## Files to modify
127	
128	| File                                                | Change                                                                                     |
129	| --------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
130	| `src/components/answer-game/types.ts`               | Add `REJECT_TAP` action                                                                    |
131	| `src/types/game-events.ts`                          | Add `expected` field to `GameEvaluateEvent`                                                |
132	| `src/components/answer-game/answer-game-reducer.ts` | Handle `REJECT_TAP` (increment retryCount)                                                 |
133	| `src/components/answer-game/useAutoNextSlot.ts`     | Add pending-placements ref, pre-validation logic                                           |
134	| `src/components/answer-game/useDraggableTile.ts`    | Pre-validate on click, shake on reject, emit event                                         |
135	| `src/components/answer-game/useTileEvaluation.ts`   | Add `expected` to all `game:evaluate` emits, add reject feedback for `reject` mode on drag |
136	
137	## Scope
138	
139	### In scope
140	
141	- Pre-validate tile on tap/click path (`lock-auto-eject` and `reject` modes)
142	- Full reject feedback (shake, sound, red flash) for `reject` mode on all
143	  input methods
144	- Ref-based pending queue to prevent stale-closure races on rapid correct taps
145	- Shake animation on bank tile for wrong tap
146	- `REJECT_TAP` reducer action
147	- `expected` field on `GameEvaluateEvent` at all emit sites
148	- Unit tests for new paths (pre-validation, queue, reducer action)
149	
150	### Not in scope
151	
152	- `lock-manual` tap behavior (unchanged — wrong tiles go to slot)
153	- `lock-manual` sequential wrong tile blocking (separate bug, tracked in
154	  [#280](https://github.com/leocaseiro/base-skill/issues/280))
155	- Keyboard/type input path (`typeTile`)
156	- SRS consumer implementation (future — this ensures the data shape is ready)
157	- iOS-specific investigation (fix is platform-agnostic)
158	
159	## Open Questions
160	
161	- **Screen reader announcements for rejected taps:** Should the shake +
162	  red flash be accompanied by an `aria-live` announcement (e.g. "Wrong
163	  tile") so screen reader users get equivalent feedback? Deferred from
164	  doc review — not blocking for initial implementation.
165
```
</details></tool-use>

_**Agent - sidechain (claude-sonnet-4-6 2026-05-01 07:26:29Z)**_

Now I have the full picture. The key issue I identified: `placeInNextSlot` in the new `useAutoNextSlot` (Task 4) returns `rejected: true` and does NOT call `placeTile` for rejected tiles. But `handleClick` in Task 5 still calls `placeInNextSlot`, which for `lock-auto-eject` mode with a wrong tile returns `rejected: true` WITHOUT having called `placeTile`. Then `handleClick` dispatches `REJECT_TAP` and emits `game:evaluate`. But `useTileEvaluation.placeTile` is also called inside `placeInNextSlot` for accepted placements — the Task 3 change to `placeTile` to dispatch `REJECT_TAP` is for drag-only paths (when placeTile is called directly from `onDrop`). Let me verify this logical split is actually correct and there is no double-dispatch for the `reject` mode tap path.

Looking at the flow:
- Click path: `handleClick` → `placeInNextSlot(tile.id)` → if pre-validation rejects, returns `{rejected:true}` WITHOUT calling `placeTile` → `handleClick` dispatches `REJECT_TAP`
- Drag path: `onDrop` → `placeTile(droppedTileId, zoneIndex)` → if wrong + reject mode, `placeTile` dispatches `REJECT_TAP`

These are separate paths — no double-dispatch. The architecture is sound.

Now let me check the `architecture docs` CLAUDE.md rule — the plan modifies files in `src/components/answer-game/` but does not mention updating `.mdx` docs.

1	import { Meta } from '@storybook/blocks';
2	
3	<Meta title="AnswerGame/Flows" />
4	
5	# AnswerGame — Event Flows
6	
7	> Source: `src/components/answer-game/`
8	>
9	> Each diagram shows the sequence of dispatches and effects triggered by a user action.
10	> Update this file when adding new dispatch chains or changing existing ones.
11	> Run `/update-architecture-docs` for guided update prompts.
12	
13	---
14	
15	## 1. Tile Placement
16	
17	Bank tile click/tap or keyboard character press.
18	
19	```mermaid
20	sequenceDiagram
21	  actor User
22	  participant Hook as useTileEvaluation
23	  participant Reducer as answerGameReducer
24	  participant Effect as useSlotBehavior (effect)
25	
26	  User->>Hook: click bank tile / press key
27	  Hook->>Reducer: PLACE_TILE { tileId, zoneIndex }
28	  Reducer-->>Reducer: move tile from bankTileIds → zones[zoneIndex].placedTileId
29	  Reducer-->>Reducer: evaluate: all zones correct?
30	  alt all zones correct
31	    Reducer-->>Effect: phase = 'round-complete'
32	    Effect->>Effect: triggerPop animation
33	  else mismatch (wrongTileBehavior = 'reject')
34	    Reducer-->>Reducer: tile not placed, bankTileIds unchanged
35	  else mismatch (wrongTileBehavior = 'lock-manual' or 'lock-auto-eject')
36	    Reducer-->>Reducer: zones[zoneIndex].isWrong = true, isLocked = true
37	    Effect->>Effect: triggerShake animation
38	  end
39	```
40	
41	---
42	
43	## 2. Wrong Tile Auto-Eject
44	
45	Only when `config.wrongTileBehavior === 'lock-auto-eject'`.
46	
47	```mermaid
48	sequenceDiagram
49	  participant Reducer as answerGameReducer
50	  participant Effect as useSlotBehavior (effect)
51	
52	  Reducer-->>Effect: zones[zoneIndex].isWrong = true
53	  Effect->>Effect: triggerShake animation (immediate)
54	  Effect->>Effect: wait 350ms
55	  Effect->>Effect: triggerEjectReturn animation
56	  Effect->>Effect: wait 1000ms
57	  Effect->>Reducer: EJECT_TILE { zoneIndex }
58	  Reducer-->>Reducer: zones[zoneIndex].placedTileId = null, isWrong = false, isLocked = false
59	  Reducer-->>Reducer: tile id returned to bankTileIds
60	```
```
</details></tool-use>

_**User (2026-05-01 07:27:06Z)**_

This session is being continued from a previous conversation that ran out of context. The summary below covers the earlier portion of the conversation.

Summary:
1. Primary Request and Intent:
   The user requested creating an implementation plan from the design spec for issue #276 (fast-tap queue fix for false error bounces on rapid Android taps). They specified using the existing git worktree at `worktrees/276-fast-tap-queue` and invoking `superpowers:writing-plans`. After the plan was written, committed, and the execution options presented, the user invoked `/ce-doc-review` to get a formal multi-persona document review of the plan before proceeding to implementation.

2. Key Technical Concepts:
   - **Stale closure race condition**: React's batched dispatches cause second tap to read stale `activeSlotIndex`/`zones` from closure
   - **Write-ahead log pattern**: Module-scoped `pendingPlacements` ref tracks claimed slots synchronously, bypassing React batching
   - **Pre-validation at bank**: Wrong tiles checked before dispatching, shake in bank instead of traveling to slot
   - **PlaceResult return type**: `{ placed: boolean; zoneIndex: number; rejected: boolean }` replaces void
   - **REJECT_TAP reducer action**: Increments retryCount only, no zone mutations
   - **expected field on GameEvaluateEvent**: Additive field for future SRS/Anki implementation
   - **Inline style manipulation for wrong-state feedback**: Direct `el.style` assignment instead of CSS class (Spec Delta)
   - **Drain condition**: Remove pending entries where `zones[entry.zoneIndex].placedTileId === entry.tileId`
   - **requestAnimationFrame** in drag-reject path to defer shake until tile reappears at full opacity
   - **Three wrongTileBehavior modes**: `lock-auto-eject` (pre-validate on tap), `lock-manual` (no pre-validation), `reject` (pre-validate all inputs)

3. Files and Code Sections:
   - **`docs/superpowers/plans/2026-05-01-fast-tap-queue.md`** (CREATED)
     - The implementation plan — 7 tasks with full TDD code blocks
     - Committed as `4e6e6f80` on branch `276-fast-tap-queue`
     - Contains Spec Delta for inline styles vs CSS class deviation

   - **`docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-01-fast-tap-queue-design.md`** (READ)
     - The design spec driving the plan
     - Defines behavior matrix for 3 wrongTileBehavior modes × 2 input methods

   - **`src/components/answer-game/useAutoNextSlot.ts`** (TO BE MODIFIED - Task 4)
     - Current: 45 lines, returns void, has isWrong guard at line 18
     - Plan: Full rewrite with module-scoped pendingPlacements, PlaceResult return, pre-validation
     - Key new code:
     ```typescript
     export interface PlaceResult {
       placed: boolean;
       zoneIndex: number;
       rejected: boolean;
     }
     let pendingPlacements: Array<{ tileId: string; zoneIndex: number }> = [];
     export const clearPendingPlacements = (): void => { pendingPlacements = []; };
     ```

   - **`src/components/answer-game/useDraggableTile.ts`** (TO BE MODIFIED - Tasks 5, 6)
     - handleClick at lines 129-132 (currently 2 lines: speakTile + placeInNextSlot)
     - Plan adds: shake throttle check, PlaceResult branching, triggerShake, inline wrong styles, REJECT_TAP dispatch, game:evaluate emit
     - Touch drag onDrop at lines 105-108 to add reject feedback with requestAnimationFrame
     - New imports needed: `playSound`, `triggerShake`, `resolveSkin`
     - `stateRef` already defined at line 29: `const stateRef = useRef(state);`

4. Errors and fixes:
   - **MD001 heading increment error**: `### Spec Delta` was h3 directly under the header block (no h2 parent). Fixed by changing to `## Spec Delta`.
   - **Coherence review false positive on stateRef**: Reviewer flagged `stateRef.current` as undefined in Task 6. This was incorrect — `stateRef` IS defined at line 29 of useDraggableTile.ts (`const stateRef = useRef(state)`). No fix needed.

   - **Red flash CSS class vs inline styles**: Spec said "add `.is-wrong` to DraggableTile CSS module" but no CSS module exists. Bank tiles use inline `tileStyle()` which CSS classes can't override without `!important`. Resolved by adding Spec Delta documenting the deviation and using direct `el.style` manipulation.

5. Problem Solving:
   - Solved the architectural question of where to store pendingPlacements (module scope vs React context) — chose module scope for simplicity

This works for the runtime case (single page), but introduces two risks: (1) If multiple AnswerGame instances coexist on a page (e.g., Storybook stories or future multi-game layouts), they share the same queue, causing cross-contamination where one game's pending placements block slots in another. The plan changes this: wrong taps in lock-auto-eject mode now return { rejected: true } from useAutoNextSlot and the tile never reaches the slot at all -- it shakes in the bank. This is a significant UX change for the most common wrong-tile behavior mode. The spec's behavior matrix confirms this is intentional ('Tap wrong tile -> Tile shakes in bank, wrong sound, no slot placement'), but the plan does not discuss the impact on existing users who are accustomed to seeing wrong tiles land and bounce. _**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-05-01 07:28:00Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="shell" data-tool-name="Bash"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Bash**</summary>
Extract JSON findings from feasibility reviewer output

```bash
python3 -c "
import json, sys
# Read all lines and find the last assistant message with the JSON findings block
findings = None
with open('/private/tmp/claude-501/-Users-leocaseiro-Sites-base-skill/53c70b90-f08a-4dd9-9260-513bf7775566/tasks/a297f43d3caeb96fa.output') as f:
    for line in f:
        try:
            d = json.loads(line)
            if d.get('type') == 'assistant':
                msg = d.get('message', {})
                for c in msg.get('content', []):
                    if c.get('type') == 'text' and '\"findings\"' in c.get('text', ''):
                        findings = c['text']
        except: pass
if findings:
    # Extract just the JSON block
    start = findings.rfind('\`\`\`json')
    end = findings.rfind('\`\`\`', start + 7) if start >= 0 else -1
    if start >= 0 and end >= 0:
        print(findings[start+7:end])
    else:
        # Try to find raw JSON object
        start = findings.rfind('{\"reviewer\"')
        if start >= 0:
            print(findings[start:])
        else:
            print('NO_JSON_BLOCK')
else:
    print('NO_FINDINGS')
" 2>/dev/null | head -200
```

```text
NO_FINDINGS
```
</details></tool-use>

```bash
python3 -c "
import json, sys
findings = None
with open('/private/tmp/claude-501/-Users-leocaseiro-Sites-base-skill/53c70b90-f08a-4dd9-9260-513bf7775566/tasks/a0af185717dd8f1d3.output') as f:
    for line in f:
        try:
            d = json.loads(line)
            if d.get('type') == 'assistant':
                msg = d.get('message', {})
                for c in msg.get('content', []):
                    if c.get('type') == 'text' and '\"findings\"' in c.get('text', ''):
                        findings = c['text']
        except: pass
if findings:
    start = findings.rfind('\`\`\`json')
    end = findings.rfind('\`\`\`', start + 7) if start >= 0 else -1
    if start >= 0 and end >= 0:
        print(findings[start+7:end])
    else:
        start = findings.rfind('{\"reviewer\"')
        if start >= 0:
            print(findings[start:])
        else:
            print('NO_JSON_BLOCK')
else:
    print('NO_FINDINGS')
" 2>/dev/null | head -200

        "Spec Delta notes: 'bank tiles use inline styles via tileStyle() (which sets background: linear-gradient(...)) and Tailwind utility classes'"
      ]
    }
  ],
  "residual_risks": [
    "The animationend listener for clearing wrong-state inline styles (Task 5) could be orphaned if the element is unmounted during the shake animation (e.g., round advances mid-shake), leaving stale inline styles if the element is recycled.",
    "The requestAnimationFrame wrapper in Task 6 drag-reject path defers the shake until after React re-renders, but if the component unmounts between the rAF call and execution (fast round transition), ref.current will be null and the shake silently fails -- acceptable but undocumented.",
    "The pending-placements drain logic filters entries where zones[entry.zoneIndex].placedTileId === entry.tileId, but if the reducer processes PLACE_TILE and then an immediate EJECT_TILE (auto-eject timer), the drain would remove the entry prematurely, potentially allowing a duplicate placement in the same slot."
  ],
  "deferred_questions": [
    "Should the 'expected' field on GameEvaluateEvent be optional (expected?: string) rather than required, given the spec describes it as a 'non-breaking additive change' but the implementation makes it required and breaks existing emit sites?",
    "Is the lock-auto-eject UX change (wrong taps shake in bank instead of landing in slot and bouncing back) validated with users, or is it an assumption that bank-side rejection is better than the existing slot-bounce feedback?"
  ]
}
```

Extract JSON findings from feasibility reviewer output

```bash
python3 -c "
import json
findings = None
with open('/private/tmp/claude-501/-Users-leocaseiro-Sites-base-skill/53c70b90-f08a-4dd9-9260-513bf7775566/tasks/a297f43d3caeb96fa.output') as f:
    for line in f:
        try:
            d = json.loads(line)
            if d.get('type') == 'assistant':
                msg = d.get('message', {})
                for c in msg.get('content', []):
                    if c.get('type') == 'text' and 'reviewer' in c.get('text', '') and 'finding' in c.get('text', '').lower():
                        txt = c['text']
                        # Find JSON block
                        start = txt.rfind('\`\`\`json')
                        end = txt.rfind('\`\`\`', start + 7) if start >= 0 else -1
                        if start >= 0 and end >= 0:
                            findings = txt[start+7:end]
                        elif '{\"reviewer\"' in txt:
                            s = txt.rfind('{\"reviewer\"')
                            findings = txt[s:]
        except: pass
if findings:
    print(findings[:8000])
else:
    print('NO_FINDINGS')
" 2>/dev/null
```
